when relevant for task load skill before use read / execute files with code_execution_tool follow instructions in skill mind relative paths conversation history discards old messages use skills_tool:load again when lost ### wait pause execution for a set time or until a timestamp use args "seconds" "minutes" "hours" "days" for duration use "until" with ISO timestamp for a specific time usage: 1 wait duration ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I need to wait..." ], "headline": "...", "tool_name": "wait", "tool_args": { "minutes": 1, "seconds": 30 } } ~~~ 2 wait timestamp ~~~json { "thoughts": [ "I will wait until..." ], "headline": "...", "tool_name": "wait", "tool_args": { "until": "2025-10-20T10:00:00Z" } } ~~~ ## "Multimodal (Vision) Agent Tools" available: ### vision_load: load image data to LLM use paths arg for attachments multiple images if needed only bitmaps supported convert first if needed **Example usage**: ```json { "thoughts": [ "I need to see the image...", ], "headline": "Loading image for visual analysis", "tool_name": "vision_load", "tool_args": { "paths": ["/path/to/image.png"], } } ``` # Available skills - skills in "**name** description" format - use skills_tool to load with **skill_name** when relevant **gchat-thread-reader** Read Google Chat conversations using a remote Chrome DevTools headless instance. Use when the user asks to check chat messages, read recent Google Chat threads, get chat conversations, or review Google Chat activity. Connects to a running Chrome instance via CDP and extracts who said what and when from each conversation. Supports DMs and group chats. **gmail-thread-reader** Read Gmail email threads using a remote Chrome DevTools headless instance. Use when the user asks to check emails, read recent email threads, get email conversations, or review inbox messages. Connects to a running Chrome instance via CDP and extracts who said what and when from each thread. Supports label-based exclusion and priority tagging. **jira-filter-listing** Fetch Jira tickets from a saved filter by filter ID. Use when the user asks to list tickets from a Jira filter, board filter, or references a filter number/ID. Resolves the filter's JQL and returns full issue details in JSON or YAML. **jira-query-listing** Fetch Jira tickets using a raw JQL query. Use when the user asks to search for tickets by JQL, project, status, assignee, labels, sprint, or any Jira search criteria. Returns full issue details including hierarchy, links, and comments. **jira-view-listing** Fetch Jira tickets from a Polaris View (Jira Product Discovery / Roadmaps). Use when the user asks about team initiatives, backlog items, roadmap tickets, sprint tasks, or anything in a specific Jira view. Resolves the view's dynamic JQL and returns full issue details including hierarchy, links, and comments. **create-skill** Wizard for creating new Agent Zero skills. Guides users through creating well-structured SKILL.md files. Use when users want to create custom skills. # Secret Placeholders - user secrets are masked and used as aliases - use aliases in tool calls they will be automatically replaced with actual values You have access to the following secrets: ## Important Guidelines: - use exact alias format `§§secret(key_name)` - values may contain special characters needing escaping in code, sanitize in your code if errors occur - comments help understand purpose # Additional variables - use these non-sensitive variables as they are when needed - use plain text values without placeholder format # Projects - user can create and activate projects - projects have work folder in /usr/projects/ and instructions and config in /usr/projects//.a0proj - when activated agent works in project follows project instructions - agent cannot manipulate or switch projects no project currently activated Human: {"user_message": "## Task: Daily Briefing — Action Items, Follow-Ups & Summary\n\nFetch data from Jira, Google Chat, and Gmail for the **last 5 days**, then analyze and present a structured briefing with **full detail** for every item.\n\n### Phase 1: Data Collection\n\nRun these steps sequentially. Capture stdout (JSON) into Python variables for analysis. Print progress to stderr.\n\n⚠️ **CRITICAL: These scripts interact with live browser sessions and APIs. Each script can take 10-20 minutes to complete.** You MUST wait for each script to fully finish and return its complete stdout JSON before moving to the next step. Do NOT timeout, skip, or move on early — incomplete data means missing action items. If a script is still running, keep waiting. Only proceed to Phase 2 after ALL scripts have completed and you have captured ALL their output.\n\n**0. Memory Recall + Past Daily Work Reports:**\nBefore running external scripts, gather context from two internal sources:\n\na) **Memory recall:** Search memory for previously stored action items, follow-ups, pending tasks, or commitments that Michael has made. Use keywords: \"action item\", \"follow-up\", \"TODO\", \"pending\", \"waiting on\", \"committed to\", \"promised\", \"deadline\".\n\nb) **Past daily work reports:** Read any existing daily work report files from the last 5 days in the \"workdir\" folder (files matching pattern `❗ Daily Work Report *.md`). Extract all action items and follow-ups from previous reports to cross-check against today's data — this ensures nothing falls through the cracks between reports.\n\nCombine both sources as additional data alongside Jira/GChat/Gmail. If a previous report's action item still appears unresolved, it must be included in today's report.\n\n**1. Jira (filter 13811):**\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/jira-filter-listing/scripts/jira_filter_listing.py --filter-id 13811 --limit 100\n```\nOutput: JSON array of issue objects. Each has: `key`, `title`, `description`, `status.name`, `status.category`, `assignee`, `priority`, `labels`, `parent`, `subtasks`, `links`, `created`, `updated`, `comments[]` (each with `author`, `body`, `created`).\n\nFilter the results client-side: only include tickets where `updated` is within the last 5 days.\n\n**2. Google Chat (last 5 days):**\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/gchat-thread-reader/scripts/gchat_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 300 --max-scroll 5\n```\nOutput: JSON array of conversation objects. Each has: `name` (person/group), `url`, `message_count`, `messages[]` (each with `sender`, `timestamp`, `epoch_ms`, `body`).\n\n**3. Gmail (last 5 days):**\n```bash\npython /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 100\n```\nOutput: JSON array of thread objects. Each has: `subject`, `thread_url`, `labels`, `priority`, `senders[]`, `last_date`, `messages[]` (each with `from`, `to[]`, `cc[]`, `date`, `body`).\n\n### Phase 2: Analysis\n\nUsing ALL collected data, identify and categorize into exactly 3 sections.\n\nCRITICAL REQUIREMENT — **Every item must include enough detail that Michael can understand the full situation without clicking any links.** Do NOT write one-line summaries. Each item must answer: what is this about, who is involved, what happened recently, and what exactly needs to happen next.\n\n#### Section 1: 🔴 My Action Items (Pending My Action)\n\nItems where **Michael must do something**. Look for:\n- Jira tickets assigned to Michael (assignee contains \"Michael\") with status NOT \"Done\"/\"Closed\"\n- Jira tickets where the latest comment explicitly asks Michael to take action, review, or approve\n- Chat/email messages where someone directly asks Michael to do something, provide input, review, or decide\n- Overdue items or items at risk of missing deadline\n\nFor each action item, present ALL of the following:\n\n| Field | What to Include |\n|-------|-----------------|\n| Source | `Jira` / `GChat` / `Gmail` / `Memory` |\n| Reference | Ticket key (e.g., DPD-644), conversation/thread name, or memory fragment ID. Include link/URL if available. |\n| What | **2-4 sentences minimum.** Describe the actual issue or request in full. What is the problem? What specifically is Michael being asked to do? Include product names, feature names, error descriptions, or business context mentioned in the source messages. |\n| Key Quote | **Quote the most relevant message verbatim** (max 2-3 sentences) that triggered this action item — the exact words from the person asking Michael to do something. Format: `\"[quote]\" — Sender Name, Date` |\n| Who Asked | Full name of the person who requested Michael's action |\n| Urgency | 🔴 Overdue/Blocker — explain why (e.g., \"blocking release\", \"SLA breach\") / 🟡 Today — explain why (e.g., \"requested today\", \"deadline today\") / 🟢 This week — explain why |\n| Related | Any related Jira tickets, threads, or conversations that provide additional context (list keys/names) |\n| Suggestion | **1-2 sentences.** Based on the context, suggest the best next action for Michael — e.g., \"Reply to Pamela with the Eurolink mapping fix ETA\", \"Schedule a 15-min call with Charlene to align on discount sync logic\", \"Escalate to infra team if root cause is not found by EOD\". Be specific and actionable. |\n\n#### Section 2: 🟡 My Follow-Ups (Track Who & What)\n\nItems where Michael is **waiting on someone else**. Look for:\n- Jira tickets where Michael left the last comment asking someone to do something\n- Chat/email threads where Michael sent the last message asking a question or requesting action\n- Items Michael delegated or requested but hasn't received a response\n\nFor each follow-up, present ALL of the following:\n\n| Field | What to Include |\n|-------|-----------------|\n| Source | `Jira` / `GChat` / `Gmail` / `Memory` |\n| Reference | Ticket key, conversation/thread name, or memory fragment ID. Include link/URL if available. |\n| Waiting On | Full name of the person Michael is waiting on |\n| What Michael Asked | **2-3 sentences minimum.** What did Michael specifically request? Include the context: what decision, deliverable, review, or information is pending. |\n| Key Quote | **Quote Michael's last message verbatim** (max 2-3 sentences) that shows what he asked for. Format: `\"[quote]\" — Michael, Date` |\n| Since | Exact date/time when Michael last asked |\n| Days Waiting | Number of days since Michael's last message (calculate from current date) |\n| Suggestion | **1-2 sentences.** Based on how long Michael has been waiting and the item's urgency, suggest the best follow-up action — e.g., \"Send a gentle nudge to Meta on GChat\", \"Escalate to Vipul's manager if no response by tomorrow\", \"This is low priority — wait until next week before following up\". Be specific and actionable. |\n\n#### Section 3: 📋 Summary of Everything Else\n\nRemaining items not in Section 1 or 2. Group by source and provide **real detail** — not one-line summaries.\n\n**Jira — Other Active Tickets:**\nFor each ticket, include:\n- `[KEY]` **Title** — Current status — Assigned to\n- **Latest update (1-2 sentences):** What changed in the last 5 days? Who updated it and what did they say/do?\n- **Suggestion:** Should Michael take note, deprioritize, or keep an eye on this? Why?\n- Skip tickets with zero activity in the last 5 days.\n\n**GChat — Informational Conversations:**\nFor each conversation, include:\n- **Thread name** (link if available)\n- **Topic summary (2-3 sentences):** What is the conversation about? What was discussed? Who are the main participants?\n- **Key takeaway:** Any decisions made, announcements shared, or outcomes reached.\n- **Suggestion:** Does Michael need to be aware of anything? Should he chime in, or is this purely FYI?\n\n**Gmail — Other Threads:**\nFor each thread, include:\n- **Subject** — From (sender names)\n- **Thread summary (2-3 sentences):** What is this email chain about? Is it FYI, an automated alert, a newsletter, or a resolved discussion?\n- **Status:** Resolved / FYI / Automated / No action needed — explain why.\n- **Suggestion:** Archive, label for later, or keep in inbox? Why?\n\n### Output Format\n\n- Use the exact section headers and emoji markers above\n- Within each section, sort by urgency (most urgent first)\n- **Be specific about WHO and WHAT** — never write vague one-liners like \"Investigate issue\" or \"Enable configuration updates\". Always include the full context.\n- If any item references a Jira ticket key (e.g., DPD-644), always include the key\n- If a section is empty, say \"None identified in the last 5 days\"\n- At the end, add: **Quick Stats:** `X action items | Y follow-ups | Z other items across {n_memory} memory items, {n_jira} Jira tickets, {n_gchat} chats, {n_gmail} emails`\n\n### Save Report\n\nAfter presenting the full briefing to Michael, save the complete report (all 3 sections + Quick Stats) as a markdown file:\n- **Path:** `workdir/❗ Daily Work Report 2026-03-14.md` (use today's date)\n- **Overwrite** if a file with the same name already exists\n- Include all sections, tables, quotes, and suggestions exactly as presented\n\n### Error Handling\n\n- If any script fails, report the error clearly and continue with the remaining scripts\n- If JIRA_EMAIL or JIRA_API_KEY env vars are missing, report and skip Jira\n- If Chrome CDP connection fails (gchat/gmail), report and skip those sources\n- Never silently skip data — always report what succeeded and what failed"} {"messages_summary": "The AI attempted to gather data from Jira, GChat, and Gmail for a daily briefing, but the process failed due to a missing playwright dependency. control product ad placement and count via a Split feature flag, \\nSo that I can optimize the homepage layout (swimlanes and vertical scroll) on both Omni and OG Homepages without code changes or manual API parameter updates.\\nAcceptance criteria\\nScenario: Dynamic API request based on Split configuration\\nGiven the feature flag for swimlanes is ON\\n\\nAnd the configuration defines the slots as [3, 5, 7]\\n\\nWhen a user navigates to the homepage\\n\\nThen the application should request 3 ads for the enabled swimlanes\\n\\nAnd render them at positions 3, 5, and 7. \\n\\n\\nScenario: Fallback to default\\nGiven the feature flag is OFF\\n\\nWhen the homepage is loaded\\n\\nThen the application should use the current slots [1,3]\\n\\nAnd the API request should fetch 2 ads for the enabled swimlanes \\n\\n\\nScenario: Changing ad density via Split \\nGiven the current Split configuration is [3, 5, 7]\\n\\nWhen I update the Split configuration to [2, 4, 6, 8, 10] in the dashboard\\n\\nAnd a new user session begins on the homepage\\n\\nThen the system should automatically request 5 ads for the enabled swimlanes from the API \\n\\nAnd the ads should render at the new specified indices without a code deployment.\\n\\n\\nScenario: Handling an empty slot configuration\\nGiven the Split configuration is set to an empty array []\\n\\nWhen the homepage is loaded\\n\\nThen the application should request 0 ads from the Ad API\\n\\nAnd the homepage should display only organic products with no ads or gaps.\\n\\n\\nScenario: Ad Supply Shortage (Data Integrity)\\nGiven the Split configuration is set to [3, 5, 7] (3 slots)\\n\\nBut the Ad API only returns 2 valid ads\\n\\nWhen the homepage renders\\n\\nThen the system should fill slots 3 and 5\\n\\nAnd slot 7 should show organic content.\\n\\n\\nScenario 4: Out-of-Bounds Handling\\nGiven the Split configuration includes a slot index of 20\\n\\nBut there are only 10 items available in the swimlane \\n\\nWhen the page renders\\n\\nThen the system should ignore the index of 20 and render only the ads that fit within the available content range.\\n\\n\\nConstraint: Integration with Existing Stock Logic\\nConstraint: The system must continue to honour the existing stock availability check prior to requesting ads. amount\",\n \"description\": \"Objective: Sales posting needs to include BCRS deposit collected from customer at an order line level\\n\\nUse-cases:\\nE-Comm item\\n\\nMarketplace item\\n\\nReturn & refund\\n\\n\\nDBP will be responsible for calculating the deposit amount for each order and interfacing this data to SAP at the order and SKU level.\\nSample deposit API is mentioned below\\nBRD DBP BCRS Requirement for Plan B\\nBCRS- Deposit API details & Test Data\\nBCRS - Sample order-v3 API\\n\\nAcceptance Criteria\\nScenario: Process only COMPLETED orders for BCRS deposit posting\\nGiven an order message is received from Order Service\\n\\nWhen the order status is COMPLETED\\n\\nThen the SAP Deposit Posting service processes the order for BCRS deposit posting\\n\\n\\nScenario: Ignore non-COMPLETED orders for BCRS deposit posting\\nGiven an order message is received from Order Service\\n\\nWhen the order status is not COMPLETED\\n\\nThen the SAP Deposit Posting service acknowledges the message\\n\\nAnd the order is not processed for BCRS deposit posting\\n\\n\\nScenario: Ignore OFFLINE and RB PreOrder orders\\nGiven an order is from an OFFLINE channel or is an RB PreOrder\\n\\nWhen the order is evaluated for deposit posting\\n\\nThen the order is ignored for BCRS deposit posting to SAP\\n\\n\\nScenario: Calculate deposit amount per BCRS-eligible line\\nGiven a BCRS-eligible order line has a deposit price per unit\\n\\nAnd the line has an ordered quantity\\n\\nWhen the deposit is calculated\\n\\nThen the line deposit amount equals deposit price per unit multiplied by the ordered quantity\\n\\nAnd the ordered quantity is based on the final fulfilled quantity as per BRD rules\\n\\n\\nScenario: Aggregate deposit amount for orders with multiple BCRS items\\nGiven an order contains multiple BCRS-eligible order lines\\n\\nWhen the SAP Deposit Posting service builds the posting payload\\n\\nThen the total BCRS deposit amount for the order equals the sum of deposit amounts of all BCRS-eligible lines\\n\\n\\nScenario: Charge deposit for donation orders\\nGiven an order is a donation order containing BCRS-eligible items\\n\\nWhen the order is processed for deposit posting\\n\\nThen deposit is calculated using the same logic as regular orders\\n\\nAnd deposit is posted to SAP for the BCRS-eligible items\\n\\n\\nScenario: Charge deposit for FOC items\\nGiven an order line is an FOC item\\n\\nAnd the master SKU is BCRS-eligible\\n\\nWhen the order is processed for deposit posting\\n\\nThen deposit is calculated using the same logic as non-FOC items\\n\\nAnd deposit is posted to SAP for the FOC item\",\n \"status\": {\n \"name\": \"IN RELASE QUEUE\",\n \"category\": \"Done\"\n },\n \"assignee\": \"Michael Bui\",\n \"priority\": \"body\": \"\"\n }\n ]\n },\n {\n \"key\": \"OPCO-1940\",\n \"id\": \"571675\",\n \"title\": \"FP VIPs encounter verification after S&G purchase for fewer reasons, vs other customers\",\n \"description\": \"Story\\nAs a FP VIP user, \\nI want to have a more seamless checkout experience when I checkout in stores using S&G, \\nso that I do not have to wait to verify my order every time or frequently.\\n\\nDetails:\\n \\n\\nOut of the rules mentioned in the document here, VIPs should not go through verification for all the rules, except\\n\\u2013 Rules which are dependent on purchase of certain restricted and high-risk products\\n\\u2013 1. Age restricted items rule\\n\\u2013 2. Session has the SKU with force verification\\nAcceptance Criteria\\nFor all scenarios below, unless otherwise specified:\\nVIP user = a customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list configured in Split (sourced from CS\\u2019 VIP list \\u2013 personal accounts only).\\n\\nNon\\u2011VIP user = a customer whose Customer ID is not present in that VIP list.\\n\\nS&G checkout = Scan & Go purchase flow in physical stores.\\n\\nRestricted / high\\u2011risk products = products that would normally trigger S&G verification per current rules (e.g., high-priced items, high fraud risk, suspicious patterns, etc.). VIPs MUST still be verified for high-priced items.\\n\\nAge\\u2011restricted items = items tagged as requiring age verification.\\n\\nForce\\u2011verification SKUs = SKUs that are explicitly configured to always require verification, regardless of other rules.\\n\\nVIP bypass logic = new logic where VIPs skip S&G verification for all rules except:\\nAge\\u2011restricted items\\n\\nForce\\u2011verification SKUs\\n\\nHigh-priced items (items with price > HighRiskPrice threshold)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n1. Common Scenarios (VIP bypass works as intended)\\n1.1 VIP user with only non\\u2011restricted items \\u2013 no verification\\nScenario 1.1 \\u2013 VIP bypass with non\\u2011restricted basket\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout in a participating store\\nAND the basket contains only non\\u2011restricted, non\\u2011high\\u2011risk items\\nAND no age\\u2011restricted items are present\\nAND no SKUs in the basket are marked as force\\u2011verification\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and reaches the normal verification decision step\\n\\nThen the system MUST bypass the S&G verification step\\nAND the customer MUST be allowed to complete payment without any S&G verification prompt\\nAND the order status MUST be the same as for a successfully verified order.\\n\\n\\n1.2 VIP user with items that would normally trigger generic risk rules \\u2013 still bypassed\\nScenario 1.2 \\u2013 VIP bypass for generic risk\\u2011based rules\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains products or patterns that would normally trigger non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced, non-high-priced S&G verification rules (e.g., abnormal basket value, frequency\\u2011based risk rule, etc.)\\nAND no age\\u2011restricted items are present\\nAND no SKUs in the basket are marked as force\\u2011verification\\nAND no high-priced items are present\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST ignore / bypass those generic S&G verification rules for this transaction\\nAND the customer MUST NOT be prompted for S&G verification\\nAND the order MUST proceed directly to completion without verification.\\n\\n\\n1.3 VIP user with age\\u2011restricted items but no force\\u2011verification SKUs \\u2013 only age verification applied\\nScenario 1.3 \\u2013 VIP still subject to age\\u2011restricted rule\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains at least one age\\u2011restricted item\\nAND the basket contains no SKUs marked as force\\u2011verification\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST still apply the age\\u2011restriction verification rule as per current logic\\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for appropriate age verification\\nAND all other non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed for this VIP user.\\n\\n\\n1.4 VIP user with force\\u2011verification SKU but no age\\u2011restricted items \\u2013 force verification applied\\nScenario 1.4 \\u2013 VIP still subject to force\\u2011verification SKUs\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains at least one SKU that is configured as force\\u2011verification\\nAND the basket contains no age\\u2011restricted items\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST still trigger S&G verification due to the force\\u2011verification SKU\\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for S&G verification as per the existing force\\u2011verification flow\\nAND all other non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed for this VIP user.\\n\\n\\n1.5 VIP user with both age\\u2011restricted and force\\u2011verification items \\u2013 verification still required\\nScenario 1.5 \\u2013 VIP with both age\\u2011restricted and force\\u2011verification SKUs\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains at least one age\\u2011restricted item\\nAND the basket contains at least one SKU configured as force\\u2011verification\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST trigger S&G verification (driven by the age\\u2011restricted rule and/or force\\u2011verification SKU)\\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for S&G verification as per the strictest applicable existing flow\\nAND all other non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed.\\n\\n\\n1.6 Non\\u2011VIP users \\u2013 behaviour unchanged\\nScenario 1.6 \\u2013 Non\\u2011VIP user retains existing verification logic\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is not present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND any combination of products is in the basket\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the S&G verification behaviour MUST remain identical to the pre\\u2011VIP\\u2011bypass implementation\\nAND the presence or configuration of the VIP Split flag MUST NOT change the verification decision for this user.\\n\\n\\n2. Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions\\n2.1 VIP Split flag disabled \\u2013 VIP bypass not applied\\nScenario 2.1 \\u2013 Feature flag disabled\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is disabled or not rolled out to this customer/environment\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND any combination of products is in the basket\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout\\n\\nThen the system MUST apply the original S&G verification rules as if the VIP bypass feature does not exist\\nAND the customer MUST be treated as a non\\u2011VIP for verification decisions.\\n\\n\\n2.2 VIP list empty in Split \\u2013 all users treated as non\\u2011VIP\\nScenario 2.2 \\u2013 Empty VIP list configuration\\nGiven the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the VIP customer ID list configured in Split is empty (no Customer IDs)\\n\\nWhen any customer (of any type) proceeds to S&G checkout\\n\\nThen no customer MUST be treated as VIP for verification purposes\\nAND all S&G verification decisions MUST follow the existing non\\u2011VIP logic.\\n\\n\\n2.3 Customer ID not resolvable \\u2013 fallback to non\\u2011VIP behaviour\\nScenario 2.3 \\u2013 Missing or unresolvable Customer ID\\nGiven a customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND due to a data or integration issue the system cannot reliably resolve the customer\\u2019s Customer ID\\nOR the Customer ID is not present in the request context where VIP evaluation is performed\\n\\nWhen the system evaluates whether the user is VIP\\n\\nThen the system MUST default to treating the user as non\\u2011VIP\\nAND apply existing S&G verification rules with no VIP bypass.\\n\\n\\n2.4 VIP list lookup failure (Split outage / timeout) \\u2013 safe fallback\\nScenario 2.4 \\u2013 Split unresponsive or returns error\\nGiven a customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the system attempts to evaluate VIP status using Split\\nAND the Split call to retrieve the VIP list or evaluate the flag fails (e.g., timeout, network error, 5xx, malformed response)\\n\\nWhen the VIP evaluation cannot be reliably completed\\n\\nThen the system MUST safely default to treating the user as non\\u2011VIP\\nAND apply existing S&G verification logic without any VIP bypass\\nAND the failure MUST be logged with sufficient details for operational monitoring (without impacting customer\\u2011facing flow beyond standard verification).\\n\\n\\n2.5 VIP user with combination of restricted and non\\u2011restricted items \\u2013 rule precedence\\nScenario 2.5 \\u2013 Mixed basket precedence for VIP\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains both:\\nnon\\u2011restricted, non\\u2011high\\u2011risk items, and\\n\\none or more items that are either age\\u2011restricted or force\\u2011verification SKUs\\n\\n\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout\\n\\nThen the presence of any age\\u2011restricted or force\\u2011verification SKU MUST take precedence\\nAND the system MUST trigger S&G verification according to the strongest applicable rule\\nAND VIP bypass MUST only apply to the remaining non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced rules.\\n\\n\\n2.6 Non\\u2011personal accounts excluded from VIP list\\nScenario 2.6 \\u2013 Only personal accounts treated as VIP\\nGiven the VIP list configured in Split is derived from CS\\u2019 list of VIP customers\\nAND it only includes personal customer accounts (as per technical notes)\\nAND a logged\\u2011in customer is using a non\\u2011personal account type (e.g., corporate / business) during S&G checkout\\n\\nWhen the system evaluates VIP status\\n\\nThen this customer MUST NOT be considered VIP based solely on a non\\u2011personal account\\nAND existing S&G verification rules MUST apply with no VIP bypass.\\n\\n\\n2.7 Multiple environments / test users \\u2013 controlled via Split\\nScenario 2.7 \\u2013 Environment and rollout control\\nGiven the VIP bypass feature is controlled via Split\\nAND specific environments or cohorts are configured in Split to include or exclude certain users as VIP\\n\\nWhen a customer proceeds to S&G checkout in any environment\\n\\nThen their VIP status MUST be determined solely based on:\\nmembership of their Customer ID in the VIP list configured for that environment/cohort in Split, and\\n\\nthe flag state for that environment/cohort\\nAND no hard\\u2011coded or environment\\u2011specific overrides outside of Split MUST be used to determine VIP status.\",\n \"status\": {\n \"name\": \"In release queue\",\n \"cate<<\n885936 CHARACTERS REMOVED TO SAVE SPACE\n>>rding\\n\\nAllen has now received a login; in parallel, Phil and I will make ourselves available to onboard him personally.     "key": "DPD-715",
    "id": "588771",
    "title": "Dynamic ad slot configuration for Homepage swimlanes",
    "description": "As a Product Manager, \nI want to control product ad placement and count via a Split feature flag, \nSo that I can optimize the homepage layout (swimlanes and vertical scroll) on both Omni and OG Homepages without code changes or manual API parameter updates.\nAcceptance criteria\nScenario: Dynamic API request based on Split configuration\nGiven the feature flag for swimlanes is ON\n\nAnd the configuration defines the slots as [3, 5, 7]\n\nWhen a user navigates to the homepage\n\nThen the application should request 3 ads for the enabled swimlanes\n\nAnd render them at positions 3, 5, and 7. \n\n\nScenario: Fallback to default\nGiven the feature flag is OFF\n\nWhen the homepage is loaded\n\nThen the application should use the current slots [1,3]\n\nAnd the API request should fetch 2 ads for the enabled swimlanes \n\n\nScenario: Changing ad density via Split \nGiven the current Split configuration is [3, 5, 7]\n\nWhen I update the Split configuration to [2, 4, 6, 8, 10] in the dashboard\n\nAnd a new user session begins on the homepage\n\nThen the system should automatically request 5 ads for the enabled swimlanes from the API \n\nAnd the ads should render at the new specified indices without a code deployment.\n\n\nScenario: Handling an empty slot configuration\nGiven the Split configuration is set to an empty array []\n\nWhen the homepage is loaded\n\nThen the application should request 0 ads from the Ad API\n\nAnd the homepage should display only organic products with no ads or gaps.\n\n\nScenario: Ad Supply Shortage (Data Integrity)\nGiven the Split configuration is set to [3, 5, 7] (3 slots)\n\nBut the Ad API only returns 2 valid ads\n\nWhen the homepage renders\n\nThen the system should fill slots 3 and 5\n\nAnd slot 7 should show organic content.\n\n\nScenario 4: Out-of-Bounds Handling\nGiven the Split configuration includes a slot index of 20\n\nBut there are only 10 items available in the swimlane \n\nWhen the page renders\n\nThen the system should ignore the index of 20 and render only the ads that fit within the available content range.\n\n\nConstraint: Integration with Existing Stock Logic\nConstraint: The system must continue to honour the existing stock availability check prior to requesting ads. No out of stock products should be shown as ads.",
    "status": {
      "name": "IN DEVELOPMENT",
      "category": "In Progress"
    },

    "key": "DPD-700",
    "id": "588466",
    "title": "Fix RMN pentest Low and optionally Info issues",
    "description": "Ref:  \n\ud83d\udfe1 Low Severity Findings\nUnreferenced Page Discovery (Open) \u2013 Audit and remove unused, hidden, or deprecated API endpoints.\n\nLack of Resources & Rate Limiting (Open) \u2013 Implement GCP Cloud Armor rate limiting and enforce payload size limits in the application code.\n\nMissing HTTP \"Strict-Transport-Security\" (HSTS) Header (Open) \u2013 Enforce HTTPS globally by adding the HSTS header via the GCP Load Balancer.\n\nTLS/SSL Timing Side-Channel Attacks (\"Lucky Thirteen\") (\u2705 Resolved) \u2013 Fixed by the new \"RESTRICTED\" GCP SSL Policy (CBC ciphers disabled).\n\nTLS/SSL Server Supports Static Key Ciphers (\u2705 Resolved) \u2013 Fixed by the new \"RESTRICTED\" GCP SSL Policy (only forward-secret ciphers allowed).\n\n\n\ud83d\udd35 Informational Severity Findings\nCacheable HTTPS Response (Open) \u2013 Add Cache-control: no-store header to prevent browsers from caching sensitive API responses.\n\nMissing \"Content-Security-Policy\" (CSP) Header (Open) \u2013 Add a strict API CSP header (e.g., default-src 'none') as defense-in-depth against injection attacks.\n\nMissing \"X-Content-Type-Options\" Header (Open) \u2013 Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header to prevent malicious MIME-type sniffing.\n\nMissing \"X-Frame-Options\" Header (Open) \u2013 Add X-Frame-Options: DENY header to prevent the API from being embedded in malicious iframes.\n\nCross-origin Resource Sharing (CORS): Arbitrary Origin Trusted (Open) \u2013 Remove wildcard (*) CORS origins; enforce a strict whitelist of trusted FairPrice domains in the Cloud Run app.\n\nMissing \"Referrer-Policy\" Header (Open) \u2013 Add Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin header to prevent leaking sensitive URL parameters to third-party sites.",
    "status": {
      "name": "Done",
      "category": "Done"
    },
    "assignee": "Michael Bui",
    "priority": "High",
    "labels": [
      "priority:improvement"
    ],

    "title": "Fix RMN pentest medium issues",
    "description": "Ref:  \nTLS/SSL Birthday Attacks on 64-bit Block Ciphers (SWEET32): Vulnerable legacy block ciphers in CBC mode.\n\nTLS/SSL Server is enabling the BEAST attack: Allows man-in-the-middle attacks via blockwise chosen-boundary attack.\n\nTLS Version 1.0 Protocol Deprecated: Accepts connections using TLS 1.0, which has design flaws.\n\nTLS Version 1.1 Protocol Deprecated: Accepts connections using TLS 1.1, lacking support for recommended cipher suites.\n\nTLS/SSL Server Supports DES and IDEA Cipher Suites: Includes outdated cipher suites based on DES and IDEA algorithms.\n\nTLS/SSL Timing Side-Channel Attacks (\"Lucky Thirteen\" Attack): Vulnerable to timing side-channel attacks on MAC check.\n\nTLS/SSL Server Supports The Use of Static Key Ciphers: Supports ciphers that do not provide \"Forward Secrecy.\"",

  {
    "key": "DPD-383",
    "id": "581187",
    "title": "Sales posting for BCRS deposit amount",
    "description": "Objective: Sales posting needs to include BCRS deposit collected from customer at an order line level\n\nUse-cases:\nE-Comm item\n\nMarketplace item\n\nReturn & refund\n\n\nDBP will be responsible for calculating the deposit amount for each order and interfacing this data to SAP at the order and SKU level.\nSample deposit API is mentioned below\nBRD DBP BCRS Requirement for Plan B\nBCRS- Deposit API details & Test Data\nBCRS - Sample order-v3 API\n\nAcceptance Criteria\nScenario: Process only COMPLETED orders for BCRS deposit posting\nGiven an order message is received from Order Service\n\nWhen the order status is COMPLETED\n\nThen the SAP Deposit Posting service processes the order for BCRS deposit posting\n\n\nScenario: Ignore non-COMPLETED orders for BCRS deposit posting\nGiven an order message is received from Order Service\n\nWhen the order status is not COMPLETED\n\nThen the SAP Deposit Posting service acknowledges the message\n\nAnd the order is not processed for BCRS deposit posting\n\n\nScenario: Ignore OFFLINE and RB PreOrder orders\nGiven an order is from an OFFLINE channel or is an RB PreOrder\n\nWhen the order is evaluated for deposit posting\n\nThen the order is ignored for BCRS deposit posting to SAP\n\n\nScenario: Calculate deposit amount per BCRS-eligible line\nGiven a BCRS-eligible order line has a deposit price per unit\n\nAnd the line has an ordered quantity\n\nWhen the deposit is calculated\n\nThen the line deposit amount equals deposit price per unit multiplied by the ordered quantity\n\nAnd the ordered quantity is based on the final fulfilled quantity as per BRD rules\n\n\nScenario: Aggregate deposit amount for orders with multiple BCRS items\nGiven an order contains multiple BCRS-eligible order lines\n\nWhen the SAP Deposit Posting service builds the posting payload\n\nThen the total BCRS deposit amount for the order equals the sum of deposit amounts of all BCRS-eligible lines\n\n\nScenario: Charge deposit for donation orders\nGiven an order is a donation order containing BCRS-eligible items\n\nWhen the order is processed for deposit posting\n\nThen deposit is calculated using the same logic as regular orders\n\nAnd deposit is posted to SAP for the BCRS-eligible items\n\n\nScenario: Charge deposit for FOC items\nGiven an order line is an FOC item\n\nAnd the master SKU is BCRS-eligible\n\nWhen the order is processed for deposit posting\n\nThen deposit is calculated using the same logic as non-FOC items\n\nAnd deposit is posted to SAP for the FOC item",
    "status": {
      "name": "IN RELASE QUEUE",
      "category": "Done"
    },
    "assignee": "Michael Bui",

        "updated": "2026-03-06T14:30:56.574+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "Technical Notes (ref): GCP PubSub by default can deliver the same message multiple times, resulting duplicate BCRS deposit postings to SAP.\nDuplicate BCRS deposit posting to SAP:\nSAP capture only the first one:\nOrder details match between DBP and SAP:\n\ncc"
      },

  {
    "key": "OPCO-1940",
    "id": "571675",
    "title": "FP VIPs encounter verification after S&G purchase for fewer reasons, vs other customers",
    "description": "Story\nAs a FP VIP user, \nI want to have a more seamless checkout experience when I checkout in stores using S&G, \nso that I do not have to wait to verify my order every time or frequently.\n\nDetails:\n \n\nOut of the rules mentioned in the document here, VIPs should not go through verification for all the rules, except\n\u2013 Rules which are dependent on purchase of certain restricted and high-risk products\n\u2013 1. Age restricted items rule\n\u2013 2. Session has the SKU with force verification\nAcceptance Criteria\nFor all scenarios below, unless otherwise specified:\nVIP user = a customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list configured in Split (sourced from CS\u2019 VIP list \u2013 personal accounts only).\n\nNon\u2011VIP user = a customer whose Customer ID is not present in that VIP list.\n\nS&G checkout = Scan & Go purchase flow in physical stores.\n\nRestricted / high\u2011risk products = products that would normally trigger S&G verification per current rules (e.g., high-priced items, high fraud risk, suspicious patterns, etc.). VIPs MUST still be verified for high-priced items.\n\nAge\u2011restricted items = items tagged as requiring age verification.\n\nForce\u2011verification SKUs = SKUs that are explicitly configured to always require verification, regardless of other rules.\n\nVIP bypass logic = new logic where VIPs skip S&G verification for all rules except:\nAge\u2011restricted items\n\nForce\u2011verification SKUs\n\nHigh-priced items (items with price > HighRiskPrice threshold)\n\n\n\n\n1. Common Scenarios (VIP bypass works as intended)\n1.1 VIP user with only non\u2011restricted items \u2013 no verification\nScenario 1.1 \u2013 VIP bypass with non\u2011restricted basket\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout in a participating store\nAND the basket contains only non\u2011restricted, non\u2011high\u2011risk items\nAND no age\u2011restricted items are present\nAND no SKUs in the basket are marked as force\u2011verification\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and reaches the normal verification decision step\n\nThen the system MUST bypass the S&G verification step\nAND the customer MUST be allowed to complete payment without any S&G verification prompt\nAND the order status MUST be the same as for a successfully verified order.\n\n\n1.2 VIP user with items that would normally trigger generic risk rules \u2013 still bypassed\nScenario 1.2 \u2013 VIP bypass for generic risk\u2011based rules\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\nAND the basket contains products or patterns that would normally trigger non\u2011age, non\u2011forced, non-high-priced S&G verification rules (e.g., abnormal basket value, frequency\u2011based risk rule, etc.)\nAND no age\u2011restricted items are present\nAND no SKUs in the basket are marked as force\u2011verification\nAND no high-priced items are present\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\n\nThen the system MUST ignore / bypass those generic S&G verification rules for this transaction\nAND the customer MUST NOT be prompted for S&G verification\nAND the order MUST proceed directly to completion without verification.\n\n\n1.3 VIP user with age\u2011restricted items but no force\u2011verification SKUs \u2013 only age verification applied\nScenario 1.3 \u2013 VIP still subject to age\u2011restricted rule\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\nAND the basket contains at least one age\u2011restricted item\nAND the basket contains no SKUs marked as force\u2011verification\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\n\nThen the system MUST still apply the age\u2011restriction verification rule as per current logic\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for appropriate age verification\nAND all other non\u2011age, non\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed for this VIP user.\n\n\n1.4 VIP user with force\u2011verification SKU but no age\u2011restricted items \u2013 force verification applied\nScenario 1.4 \u2013 VIP still subject to force\u2011verification SKUs\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\nAND the basket contains at least one SKU that is configured as force\u2011verification\nAND the basket contains no age\u2011restricted items\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\n\nThen the system MUST still trigger S&G verification due to the force\u2011verification SKU\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for S&G verification as per the existing force\u2011verification flow\nAND all other non\u2011age, non\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed for this VIP user.\n\n\n1.5 VIP user with both age\u2011restricted and force\u2011verification items \u2013 verification still required\nScenario 1.5 \u2013 VIP with both age\u2011restricted and force\u2011verification SKUs\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\nAND the basket contains at least one age\u2011restricted item\nAND the basket contains at least one SKU configured as force\u2011verification\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\n\nThen the system MUST trigger S&G verification (driven by the age\u2011restricted rule and/or force\u2011verification SKU)\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for S&G verification as per the strictest applicable existing flow\nAND all other non\u2011age, non\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed.\n\n\n1.6 Non\u2011VIP users \u2013 behaviour unchanged\nScenario 1.6 \u2013 Non\u2011VIP user retains existing verification logic\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is not present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\nAND any combination of products is in the basket\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\n\nThen the S&G verification behaviour MUST remain identical to the pre\u2011VIP\u2011bypass implementation\nAND the presence or configuration of the VIP Split flag MUST NOT change the verification decision for this user.\n\n\n2. Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions\n2.1 VIP Split flag disabled \u2013 VIP bypass not applied\nScenario 2.1 \u2013 Feature flag disabled\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the VIP Split flag is disabled or not rolled out to this customer/environment\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\nAND any combination of products is in the basket\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout\n\nThen the system MUST apply the original S&G verification rules as if the VIP bypass feature does not exist\nAND the customer MUST be treated as a non\u2011VIP for verification decisions.\n\n\n2.2 VIP list empty in Split \u2013 all users treated as non\u2011VIP\nScenario 2.2 \u2013 Empty VIP list configuration\nGiven the VIP Split flag is enabled\nAND the VIP customer ID list configured in Split is empty (no Customer IDs)\n\nWhen any customer (of any type) proceeds to S&G checkout\n\nThen no customer MUST be treated as VIP for verification purposes\nAND all S&G verification decisions MUST follow the existing non\u2011VIP logic.\n\n\n2.3 Customer ID not resolvable \u2013 fallback to non\u2011VIP behaviour\nScenario 2.3 \u2013 Missing or unresolvable Customer ID\nGiven a customer is using S&G checkout\nAND due to a data or integration issue the system cannot reliably resolve the customer\u2019s Customer ID\nOR the Customer ID is not present in the request context where VIP evaluation is performed\n\nWhen the system evaluates whether the user is VIP\n\nThen the system MUST default to treating the user as non\u2011VIP\nAND apply existing S&G verification rules with no VIP bypass.\n\n\n2.4 VIP list lookup failure (Split outage / timeout) \u2013 safe fallback\nScenario 2.4 \u2013 Split unresponsive or returns error\nGiven a customer is using S&G checkout\nAND the system attempts to evaluate VIP status using Split\nAND the Split call to retrieve the VIP list or evaluate the flag fails (e.g., timeout, network error, 5xx, malformed response)\n\nWhen the VIP evaluation cannot be reliably completed\n\nThen the system MUST safely default to treating the user as non\u2011VIP\nAND apply existing S&G verification logic without any VIP bypass\nAND the failure MUST be logged with sufficient details for operational monitoring (without impacting customer\u2011facing flow beyond standard verification).\n\n\n2.5 VIP user with combination of restricted and non\u2011restricted items \u2013 rule precedence\nScenario 2.5 \u2013 Mixed basket precedence for VIP\nGiven a logged\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\nAND the basket contains both:\nnon\u2011restricted, non\u2011high\u2011risk items, and\n\none or more items that are either age\u2011restricted or force\u2011verification SKUs\n\n\n\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout\n\nThen the presence of any age\u2011restricted or force\u2011verification SKU MUST take precedence\nAND the system MUST trigger S&G verification according to the strongest applicable rule\nAND VIP bypass MUST only apply to the remaining non\u2011age, non\u2011forced rules.\n\n\n2.6 Non\u2011personal accounts excluded from VIP list\nScenario 2.6 \u2013 Only personal accounts treated as VIP\nGiven the VIP list configured in Split is derived from CS\u2019 list of VIP customers\nAND it only includes personal customer accounts (as per technical notes)\nAND a logged\u2011in customer is using a non\u2011personal account type (e.g., corporate / business) during S&G checkout\n\nWhen the system evaluates VIP status\n\nThen this customer MUST NOT be considered VIP based solely on a non\u2011personal account\nAND existing S&G verification rules MUST apply with no VIP bypass.\n\n\n2.7 Multiple environments / test users \u2013 controlled via Split\nScenario 2.7 \u2013 Environment and rollout control\nGiven the VIP bypass feature is controlled via Split\nAND specific environments or cohorts are configured in Split to include or exclude certain users as VIP\n\nWhen a customer proceeds to S&G checkout in any environment\n\nThen their VIP status MUST be determined solely based on:\nmembership of their Customer ID in the VIP list configured for that environment/cohort in Split, and\n\nthe flag state for that environment/cohort\nAND no hard\u2011coded or environment\u2011specific overrides outside of Split MUST be used to determine VIP status.",
    "status": {
      "name": "In release queue",
      "category": "Done"
    },
    "assignee": "Michael Bui",
    "priority": "High",
    "labels": [
      "priority:improvement"
    ],

    "parent": null,

    "comments": [

      {
        "created": "2026-01-12T16:23:25.021+0800",
        "updated": "2026-01-12T16:44:31.358+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "Split.IO Feature Flag:\nName: sng_vip_bypass\n\nTreatment JSON content: {\"vip_customer_ids\": [123, 456, 789]}\n\n\nHigh Level Logic:\ngraph TD\n    OrderReceived[Order Received] --> MandatoryChecks[Mandatory Checks]\n    MandatoryChecks --> CheckAgeRestricted{Age-Restricted?}\n    CheckAgeRestricted -->|Yes| ManualCheck[Manual Check]\n    CheckAgeRestricted -->|No| CheckHighPriced{High-Priced?}\n    CheckHighPriced -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckHighPriced -->|No| CheckForceVerificationSKU{Force-Verification SKU?}\n    CheckForceVerificationSKU -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckForceVerificationSKU -->|No| CheckVIPCustomer{VIP Customer?}\n    CheckVIPCustomer -->|Yes| AutoApprove[Auto-Approve]\n    CheckVIPCustomer -->|No| GenericVerificationRules[Generic Verification Rules]\n    GenericVerificationRules --> AnyRuleTriggered{Any Rule Triggered?}\n    AnyRuleTriggered -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    AnyRuleTriggered -->|No| AutoApproveDetailed Logic:\ngraph TD\n    OrderReceived[Order Received] --> CheckPlasticBag{Plastic Bag?}\n    CheckPlasticBag -->|Yes| AutoApprove[Auto-Approve]\n    CheckPlasticBag -->|No| CheckStoreExcluded{Store Excluded?}\n    CheckStoreExcluded -->|Yes| AutoApprove\n    CheckStoreExcluded -->|No| CheckHighValue{High-Value Items?}\n    CheckHighValue -->|Yes| ManualCheck[Manual Check]\n    CheckHighValue -->|No| CheckAgeRestricted{Age-Restricted Items?}\n    CheckAgeRestricted -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckAgeRestricted -->|No| CheckForceVerificationSKU{Force Verification SKU?}\n    CheckForceVerificationSKU -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckForceVerificationSKU -->|No| CheckVIPCustomer{VIP Customer?}\n    CheckVIPCustomer -->|Yes| AutoApprove\n    CheckVIPCustomer -->|No| CheckFirstOrder{First Order?}\n    CheckFirstOrder -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckFirstOrder -->|No| CheckRecentSessionsFailed{Recent Sessions Failed?}\n    CheckRecentSessionsFailed -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckRecentSessionsFailed -->|No| CheckSessionItemLimits{Session Item Limits Exceeded?}\n    CheckSessionItemLimits -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckSessionItemLimits -->|No| CheckRecentlyUnblocked{Customer Recently Unblocked?}\n    CheckRecentlyUnblocked -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckRecentlyUnblocked -->|No| CheckFrequentCartChanges{Frequent Cart Changes?}\n    CheckFrequentCartChanges -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckFrequentCartChanges -->|No| CheckTrustScore{Trust Score Flag = Y?}\n    CheckTrustScore -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckTrustScore -->|No| CheckRandomSampling{Random Sampling Hit?}\n    CheckRandomSampling -->|Yes| ManualCheck\n    CheckRandomSampling -->|No| AutoApprove"
      }
    ]
  },

    "key": "DPD-220",
    "id": "540010",
    "title": "Automate sync of newly onboarded SKUs into OSMOS for faster activation",
    "description": "As Advertiser and Ad Ops User,\nI want all newly listed advertisable SKUs to be available in FPG Ads Platform (OSMOS) within 24 hours of listing,\nSo that I can create the campaign on them as soon as possible and drive visibility and sales to those new SKUs.",
    "status": {

      "category": "Done"
    },

      "priority:improvement"

    },
    "subtasks": [],

    "updated": "2026-01-26T21:47:13.888+0800",
    "comments": [
      {
        "created": "2026-01-26T13:36:36.775+0800",
        "updated": "2026-01-26T13:36:36.775+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "Verified one more time before sending approval request for PRD deployment:\n \nFiles are being created every hour (with snapshots) as expected: \nConfirmed with logs:"
      },
      {
        "created": "2026-01-22T15:02:14.966+0800",
        "updated": "2026-01-22T15:02:14.966+0800",
        "author": "Nikhil Grover",
        "body": "will let you take the call on the rollout, since we cannot do the UAT \n\nthanks!"
      },
      {
        "created": "2026-01-22T14:49:24.656+0800",
        "updated": "2026-01-22T14:49:24.656+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "The risk to our production involves missing SKU mapping for existing products in Osmos, which will cause those products to disappear from campaigns and advertisers' accounts.\nTo control the risk:\nWe have implemented a sanity check to confirm that the product count is at least 500,000. \n\nAfter production deployment and job execution, I will also need to verify that the generated file is correct and compare it with the previous file, ensuring that approximately 500,000 products match between the two files.\n\nFor every file that is synced to Osmos, we have a snapshot backup with the timestamp so that we can revert to the previous version easily."
      },
      {
        "created": "2026-01-22T14:30:46.883+0800",
        "updated": "2026-01-22T14:30:46.883+0800",
        "author": "Nikhil Grover",
        "body": "as discussed, i can only sign off on the testing. \nsince we cannot test, can you share what\u2019s the risk of proceeding to production without the UAT signoff? \nthanks!"
      },
      {
        "created": "2026-01-22T11:17:14.817+0800",
        "updated": "2026-01-22T11:17:14.817+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "As I have explained, we don't have a UAT environment for this because the data source is BigQuery, which contains only production data. We cannot modify production data for UAT testing purposes. The current implemented quality gate requires that the number of SKUs in the SYNC file exceeds 500,000 (auto), and the match between the SYNC file and the latest file should also be around 500,000 (manually done during SIT, see the previous comment).\nPlease confirm if we can proceed with the production deployment. The steps for production deployment include:\nCreate the service account.\n\nSend an access request to BigQuery for the service account.\n\nSend an access request to the Osmos GCS packet for the service account.\n\nDeploy the job and ensure end-to-end functionality.\n\n\ncc"
      },
      {
        "created": "2026-01-12T17:07:36.147+0800",
        "updated": "2026-01-12T17:07:36.147+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "please let me know if you need any help with UAT. This ticket has been pending UAT (as your request) for almost a month!"
      },
      {
        "created": "2025-12-18T13:18:10.974+0800",
        "updated": "2025-12-18T13:18:10.974+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "Snapshots are also created properly for each run:"
      },
      {
        "created": "2025-12-18T12:44:33.759+0800",
        "updated": "2025-12-18T12:45:57.141+0800",
        "author": "Michael Bui",
        "body": "Files are generated in GCS bucket:\n\uf432 gsutil ls -la gs://fpg-spotlight-osmos-preprod/sku-vendor-mapping/\n  28929598  2025-12-18T03:51:20Z  gs://fpg-spotlight-osmos-preprod/sku-vendor-mapping/sku-vendor-mapping-20251218-035120.tsv#1766029880953624  metageneration=1\n  28929598  2025-12-18T03:51:21Z  gs://fpg-spotlight-osmos-preprod/sku-vendor-mapping/sku-vendor-mapping.tsv#1766029881451900  metageneration=1\nTOTAL: 2 objects, 57859196 bytes (55.18 MiB)Content match with latest PRD data with more than 500k rows (very high confident the generated file has correct content): \n \nSuccessful logs captured in Datadog:"
      }
    ]
  },

  {
    "key": "OMNI-1297",
    "id": "502092",
    "title": "Near Real-time Inventory Indexing for FFS to Decrease OOS Impressions on Search",
    "description": "Problem Definition\nWhat problem/opportunity do the customers face that you are trying to solve?\nCustomers frequently encounter discrepancies in product availability and information. A common complaint is that products shown as \u201cout of stock\u201d online are actually available in-store. Similarly, incorrect or outdated product tagging (e.g. dietary info, price, secondary category) impacts browsing, sorting, and personalization quality.\nThis affects:\nCustomer trust in stock and pricing information\n\nSearch and discovery relevance, especially during campaigns\n\nOperational productivity of the Backoffice (DBP) and Tech teams, due to frequent manual overrides and support tickets\n\nRevenue opportunities, particularly during high-traffic events like CNY or Xmas\n\n\nAs a FairPrice app/web user,\nWhen I browse or search for products\nI want to see up-to-date stock levels, prices, and tags\nSo I can confidently choose and purchase available items\n\ud83e\uddd1\u200d\ud83e\udd1d\u200d\ud83e\uddd1 Who Faces This Problem and Its Importance\nAffected Users:\nOnline and in-store customers using the app or website for search/browsing\n\nDBP (Backoffice) operators who update product metadata\n\nTech team supporting urgent data fix tickets\n\n\nDuring CNY\n% Impression on OOS\npage_type\n\nweek\ncategory\nsearch\n1\n2.18%\n2.97%\n2\n2.84%\n3.36%\n3\n2.66%\n3.18%\n4\n3.32%\n3.56%\n5\n6.18%\n8.85%\n6\n4.06%\n4.79%\n7\n2.92%\n3.40%\nEstimated Impact:\nGMV impact of $1.72M\n\nImprove customer experience NPS\n\n\nImportance:\nHigh \u2014 impacts trust, conversion, cart additions, and operational efficiency\n\nRelevance and freshness of content is core to any modern retail platform\n\n\nCurrent Workarounds:\nManual fixes via Backoffice\n\nAd hoc alerts and re-indexing requests\n\nFull-table scans every 1 hours, incurring cost and latency\n\n\nSuccess Criteria",
    "status": {
      "name": "Backlog",
      "category": "To Do"
    },
    "assignee": "Prajney Sribhashyam",
    "priority": "High",
    "labels": [],
    "duedate": null,
    "parent": null,
    "subtasks": [],
    "links": [
      {
        "type": "Polaris work item link",
        "key": "PRDM-239"
      }
    ],

    "updated": "2026-01-19T11:46:36.520+0800",
    "comments": [

        "body": "Available to promise - Estimated annual GMV impact $1M-$1.25M vs Effort estimate - Small\nNear real time inventory - Estimated annual GMV impact ~$1.5M vs Effort estimate - Medium to large\n\nCan deprioritise from pre-holiday must have list"

He requested Onkar Bamane to confirm this behavior for rows 4 and 5 in the sheet.\n*\nProject & Development Updates\n*\n*\nNew High Priority Jira Work Item (DPD-715)\n*\nA new high-priority story,\n*\nDynamic ad slot configuration for Omni homepage swimlanes\n*\n(DPD-715), was created and assigned to you.\n-\n*\nObjective:\n*\nThe goal is to allow Product Managers to control ad placement and count on the homepage using a Split feature flag, enabling optimization without code deployments.\n-\n*\nFunctionality Highlights:\n*\nThe criteria mandate dynamic functionality based on the Split configuration, including scenarios for requesting specific ad counts (e.g., requesting 3 ads for slots [3, 5, 7]), falling back to defaults if the flag is off (\n`\n[1, 3]\n`\n), and gracefully handling empty configurations (0 ads requested) or ad supply shortages.         "body": "Hi all, sharing key insights and findings following launch of\nSearch on Omni home.\nRecommendation: Proceed with 100% rollout\n, i.e. on Android app coming Monday\n1. Observed changes in customer behaviour\na. Search for EC starts 1 level up\nbut no significant uplift in upper funnel engagement metrics (Searches per user, Impressions, CTR%, ATC%).\nb. Overall upper funnel search traffic increase to a new baseline\ndue to more in-store shoppers (NCs) searching although not converting.\n4. At launch, RMN revenue from Search increased by +$845/week, but tapered off after 3 weeks\n-- To new baseline of avg ~$300 per week uplift, given higher search traffic overall.\n5.\u00a0Cost to serve increased by $68 in immediate week at launch\n,\nbut dropped to avg ~$20/week in subsequent weeks.\n-- This is directly proportional to number of searches (due to higher overall search traffic from NCs = higher # searches), and correlates to RMN revenue uplift.\n, 1 reply\n, Last Reply:\n1 unread1 reply, Last Reply Yesterday 8:58\u202fAM\n[quoted] Hi @all Ecom Search on Omni Home (FYI)\nWe had enabled search on omni home as an experiment, with iOS as Treatment while Android kept as Control group. \n\nWe will be continuing the experiment for another week until 25 Feb before concluding on the next steps, as results so far are not conclusive. \n\ncc @Daryl Chin @Sanjana Sanjana @Terence Cheah"

        "body": "You got an email\u200b from Miguel Ho Xian Da (miguel_ho@fairpricegroup.sg)\u200b:\nThe email thread discusses the operational challenge of integrating Retail Media Network content across new Fairprice touchpoints (Smart Carts, Digital Price Cards, and IPOS) which currently use separate Content Management Systems, requiring the Accenture team to assess Osmos integration capabilities and recommend a unified solution.\n*\nUpdates\n*\n-\nKenneth Teo connected Miguel Ho with Tong Yu and Mihir Shah on Tuesday, March 3, 2026, to discuss RMN requirements and noted that Tong and Mihir are working closely with the Omni team.\n-\nMiguel Ho informed the team on Tuesday, March 10, 2026, that Fairprice is scaling Smart Carts, Digital Price Card, and IPOS (new POS) to more stores this year, serving as additional retail media touchpoints beyond digital signage.\n-\nA key challenge is that each of these new touchpoints currently utilizes its own content management system, creating an operational challenge.\n*\nYour Tasks\n*\n-\nYou, Michael Bui, and the RMN team are requested by Miguel Ho to chip in if required.\n*\nOther Tasks\n*\n-\nThe Accenture team must determine if Osmos has the capability to directly publish content to the new touchpoints (Smart Carts, Digital Price Card, IPOS) via API integration or other methods, given that a content library is necessary due to different screen specifications.\n-\nThe Accenture team must recommend an alternative solution if the required Osmos content publishing capability is not possible.\n*\nSummary\n*\n-\nThe highest priority is addressing the operational challenge caused by new retail media touchpoints (Smart Carts, Digital Price Cards, IPOS) having distinct Content Management Systems, complicating content distribution.\n-\nAccenture's primary action item is to evaluate Osmos for API integration with these various touchpoints to centralize content publishing, or propose an alternative centralized solution.\n-\nThe conversation was initiated by Kenneth Teo on March 3, 2026, to facilitate discussions between Fairprice (Miguel Ho) and Accenture's Omni team (Tong Yu, Mihir Shah)     "thread_url": "https://mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#search/newer_than%3A5d/FMfcgzQfCMvDvjdnNFRlFsHGnlRCKqzh",

    "priority": null,

      {

        "to": [

            "name": "me",
            "email": "michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"
          }
        ],
        "cc": [],
        "date": "Mar 13, 2026, 9:01\u202fAM",
        "body": "Download\n\nHi Fairmily!\n\nIn celebration of International Women\u2019s Day, we shine the spotlight on branch managers Jennifer and Rachael, two of the many women in FPG who are Beating Their Best in their day-to-day work.\u00a0\nDownload\nDownload\nSince joining FairPrice Group in 2020, Rachael has redefined what it means to Beat Your Best.\n\nHer journey began during the pandemic, managing our Finest stores at Changi Airport Terminal 3 and Jewel Changi Airport. With closed borders and zero tourists, hitting sales targets seemed impossible.\nDownload\nHowever, Rachael and her team refused to be deterred, coming up with bold ideas such as curating locally flavoured products and even promoting the store on their personal RedNote accounts to attract tourists. Within just six months of borders reopening, they turned the tide and achieved a positive operating result.\u00a0\nFor Rachael, the goal isn't just about hitting a number; it\u2019s about constant growth. \u201cI never just want to hit my targets,\u201d she shared. \u201cI want to stretch myself and my team to see how much more we can achieve this month, and how we can do even better next month.\u201d\nThis drive for progress led her to champion the pilot of our first Smart Carts at FairPrice Finest Sengkang Grand Mall. While introducing new technology to customers came with its own hurdles, Rachael leaned into the challenge with excitement. She took immense pride in being at the forefront of retail innovation in Singapore, guiding her team through the transition with unwavering resolve.\nRachael credits her growth to a simple but powerful philosophy:\nDownload\nDownload\nThroughout her 19-year career\u00a0with FPG, Jennifer has consistently proven that no challenge is too big when approached with a 'can-do' spirit.\nWhile managing FairPrice Xtra Sports Hub, Jennifer faced the arrival of a nearby competitor. Instead of backing down, she hit the ground running, collaborating with product, supplier and marketing teams to launch promotional activities such as a Tsukiji Market-inspired event. From live bluefin tuna cutting shows to exclusive seafood promotions, her strategy didn't just protect sales; it drove a 3.3% year-over-year increase.\n\nJennifer\u2019s dedication to excellence also extends to how we treat our customers.\u00a0\nLeading the Net Promoter Score (NPS) workstream for Hyper, she championed simple yet powerful changes to delight customers, like implementing \"Good Morning\" and \"Thank You\" greetings at store exits. By conducting weekly power briefings across branches to keep customer experience top of mind, she helped lift the overall NPS score from 38 to 45.\u00a0\nJennifer leads by example with her infectious energy and a 'team-first' mindset. From managing complex live events to rolling kimbap on the front lines, she is always ready to dive in and help. For her, success is a team sport fuelled by grit and a shared commitment to excellence.\nDownload\nDownload\nWhether it's embracing growth opportunities or innovating new ways to improve the customer experience, Rachael and Jennifer demonstrate that when we strive for excellence, we can truly Make Every Day A Little Better for everyone around us.\nDownload\nWe are building a culture of Growth Mindset In Action, which will define how we work as we transform our organisation for the future.\n\nKnow a hero whom you want us to feature? Submit your request here.\nDownload"

        "date": "Mar 13, 2026, 3:49\u202fAM",
        "body": "Notes from \u201cDiscussion on RMN CMS for smart cart\u201d\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\nOpen meeting notes\nThe content was auto-generated on March 13, 2026, 11:48 AM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\nSummary\nRMN Centralized CMS feasibility examined architectural needs with a focus on Osmos integration for smart card deployment.\n\nAssess Centralized CMS Feasibility\nThe meeting aimed to assess the technical possibility of a centralized Content Management System for various touch points like smart card, utilizing current retail media network assets. Technical assessment is required quickly to define architecture before funding or resource commitments are made for the project.\n\nSmart Card Integration Protocol\nDiscussion centered on integrating the smart card system, confirming that the preferred method is a push API from Osmos, which should act as the source of truth for content, rules, and instructions. The current smart card production CMS can receive media via HTTP URL retrieval or SFTP content push.\n\nHandling Interactive and Offline Displays\nIt was agreed that interactive displays like smart card and I-POS should connect directly to Osmos for real-time ad serving, supporting native URLs for ad clicks. Offline mode is necessary for non-interactive screens, such as the smart card welcome screen, requiring specific API documentation for content caching and offline play capabilities.\n\nSuggested next steps\n\t[Michael Bui] Define Architecture: Assess the technical possibility and feasibility of implementing a centralized Content Management System. Collaborate with Tina, Henry, and Lens to define the architectural diagram for system integration.\n\t[Henry Huang, Tina Zhu, Len Guo] Share API Specs: Prepare full API documentation and specifications for the Smart Card system. Share the details, including SFTP requirements and data availability, with Michael Bui for review.\n\t[Miguel Ho Xian Da] Create Chat Group: Create a technical chat group for Michael Bui, Henry, and Len using Google Chat.\n\t[Miguel Ho Xian Da, Michael Bui] Expedite Development: Highlight the need to expedite development of the Osmo system scheduling and device management capability. Discuss this prioritization with Brian and Sean.\n\tNotes by Gemini\nIs the Next Steps section in this email helpful?\nGoogle LLC, 1600 Ampitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA\nYou have received this email because meeting artifacts were initiated in Google Meet."

        "date": "Mar 12, 2026, 5:31\u202fPM",
        "body": "Hi Michael,\n\nAxelera AI will be at\u00a0 ISC West, and we would love to connect with you there.\n\n\u00a0\n\nOur team will be showcasing how a precision-engineered AIPU turns AI inferencing at the edge from a promise into a practical, scalable reality.\n\n\u00a0\n\nWhat to Expect - How AI Inferencing Brings Real ROI\n\nAxelera & Digica/Innowise/Spanidea/Synthera: CrowdView - Real-Time AI for Video Analytics Real-time person identification, face matching, and crowd monitoring across high-resolution video streams. Multiple edge AI capabilities running simultaneously at the edge, no cloud required.\n\nAxelera & Kudelski: KLARQ Robot Dog Autonomous robotic patrol with onboard object detection, facial recognition, and visual command processing. Identifies threats, recognizes personnel, and responds to visual cues without cloud dependency.\n\nAxelera & WebOccult: AI Yard Monitoring and Automation Suite Advance container operations with code recognition, automated yard functions, and continuous tracking. Reduce errors, cut idle time, and deliver reliable data for faster supply chain decisions.\n\nAxelera & Spanidea: Four Minds, One Chip Four independent AI pipelines running simultaneously on a single Metis chip, combining detection, classification, segmentation, PPE compliance, defect detection, and fall detection in one system.\n\nAxelera & DeGirum: Edge-Powered Face Intelligence Real-time face detection and recognition running entirely at the edge. DeGirum's hardware-optimized platform delivers instant identification across multiple streams with zero cloud dependency.\n\nEuropa Vision Lab Next-generation hardware processing 8K video in real time, detecting the smallest objects at over 600 frames per second across 16 simultaneous streams. Built for demanding visual intelligence workloads.\n\nWhen & Where\n\nDates: March 25-27, 2026\nLocation: The Venetian Expo, Las Vegas, USA. Booth No. 31087\n\nWhether you\u2019re exploring edge AI for the first time or looking to scale deployment across real-world applications, we\u2019d be happy to walk through your use case and share what we\u2019re seeing across industries.\n\n\u00a0\n\nLet's Connect\n\nBook a Meeting Now\n\n\u00a0\n\nWe look forward to seeing you there!\n\n\u00a0\n\nThe Axelera AI Team\n\nAxelera AI B.V., High Tech Campus 5, Eindhoven, Noord-Brabant 5656 AE, Netherlands\n\nUnsubscribe Manage preferences"
      }

        "from": "Bitbucket <info@e.atlassian.com>",
        "to": [
          {
            "name": "me",
            "email": "michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"

        ],
        "cc": [],
        "date": "Mar 12, 2026, 5:30\u202fPM",
        "body": "App passwords deprecation reminder\n\nYou will need to update your app passwords to API tokens in order not to affect your current workflows\nWe're reaching out to remind you about the change with Bitbucket\u00a0Cloud's app passwords, effective June\u00a09th,\u00a02026. After this date, all app passwords will stop functioning. Many Bitbucket\u00a0Cloud users currently rely on app passwords for integrations. To enhance security, Atlassian is standardizing authentication methods across all products.\nAs part of this effort, we're replacing app passwords with API tokens. These offer a more secure, manageable, and standardized way to authenticate.\nWhat you need to know:\n\n\u2022\tCheck if you're affected: go to App passwords under Access Management in your Personal Bitbucket settings to identify which integrations or scripts currently use them.\n\n\u2022\tTake action: before June\u00a09th,\u00a02026, generate Bitbucket scoped API tokens, update your scripts or tools with the new tokens, and test your workflows.\n\n\u2022\tWhy it matters: API tokens provide finer control with scoped permissions and expiration dates, while also providing stronger security when compared to app passwords.\n\nMoving forward, you can use API tokens for all your authentication needs. To learn more about managing API tokens, please review this documentation.\nThank you for being a valued member of the Bitbucket community!\n\nLearn\u00a0more\n\nBest regards,\nThe Bitbucket\u00a0team\n\nRead\u00a0in\u00a0browser \u2022 Privacy\u00a0policy \u2022 Contact\u00a0us \u2022 Read\u00a0our\u00a0blog \u2022 Join\u00a0Atlassian\u00a0Community\n\n \u00a0  \u00a0 \n\nCopyright 2026 Atlassian Pty Ltd. All rights reserved.\nWe are located at 341 George Street, Sydney, NSW, 2000, Australia\n\nIf you no longer wish to receive these emails, please update your preferences or unsubscribe\u00a0here."
      }

    "last_date": "Thu, Mar 12, 2026, 9:00\u202fAM",
    "messages": [
      {
        "from": "HR Announcement <hr@fairpricegroup.sg>",
        "to": [
          {
            "name": "me",
            "email": "michael.bui@fairpricegroup.sg"
          }
        ],
        "cc": [],
        "date": "Mar 12, 2026, 9:00\u202fAM",
        "body": "Download\n\nDear Colleagues,\u00a0\n\nWe wish to inform you that the Code of Conduct has been updated to reflect FPG\u2019s commitment to protecting sensitive information and maintaining a high level of integrity across the Group.\n\nTo support our shared success and maintain the standards\u00a0set, we would like to highlight\u00a0important guidelines\u00a0on Confidentiality, Social Media, and External Communications. For further details, please refer to Sections C and E of the Code of Conduct here.\n\nDownload\n\nAs part of your work, you may handle Confidential Information\u00a0including\u00a0trade secrets, intellectual property,\u00a0personal data and more. We all play a part in keeping this data safe.\n\nTo keep our information secure, please remember:\n\nUse it for its intended purpose: Only access data for legitimate business needs.\n\nKeep it internal: Never share sensitive details with unauthorised parties or use them for personal gain.\n\nUse approved tools: Avoid storing company data on personal cloud services or entering it into unapproved AI platforms.\n\nSpot something wrong?\u00a0If you lose a device or suspect a data leak, please don't wait. Report it immediately to your Manager and the Cybersecurity Team so we can support you and protect the business.\n\nDownload\n\nOur reputation is built on the trust of our customers and the hard work of our teams. While it is great to share about your work online, it\u2019s important to remember that our responsibility to protect FPG's reputation\u00a0extends to social media platforms.\n\nTo help us maintain a secure and professional\u00a0presence, please follow these guidelines:\n\nProtect our workspace: Do not post photos, screenshots, or videos that show internal systems, dashboards, or background data. Even a casual \"desk selfie\" can inadvertently reveal sensitive information.\n\nSeparate\u00a0personal from the professional: It is important to keep\u00a0personal opinions distinct from work-related\u00a0communications.\u00a0If your social media profile mentions your affiliation with\u00a0FPG, do ensure it\u2019s clear that your views and opinions are your own.\n\nNot sure if a post is okay? If you are ever uncertain about sharing something online or accidentally post something sensitive, please reach out to your Manager\u00a0for guidance.\n\nThese updates help maintain a secure, ethical\u00a0and professional workplace while protecting our key strategic assets.\n\nIf you have any queries, please raise a ticket and our Care Centre will be happy to assist you."
      }
    ]
  },
