        "date": "Mar 12, 2026, 8:57\u202fAM",
        "body": "Notes from \u201cFPG : Onsite programmatic\u201d\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\nOpen meeting notes\nThe content was auto-generated on March 12, 2026, 4:56 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\nSummary\nProgrammatic ad delivery solutions were discussed with FPJ prioritizing flexible ad dimensions with the Osmos SDK not yet supporting required features for a Q2 end go-live date, prompting consideration of a temporary API integration.\n\nProgrammatic Ad Delivery Scope\nThe discussion centered on finalizing programmatic ad delivery via Osmos, focusing on rendering ads on the front end and accurately tracking impression and click events, including managing necessary consents and ad format guidelines. IAB sizes from programmatic demand were noted as a limitation, though the FPJ required a non-negotiable 2x1 dimension for customer experience.\n\nRendering and Tracking Approach\nThe preferred approach involves FPJ's internal implementation rendering the ads based on information returned by the API, with the upgraded Osmos SDK being used solely for tracking purposes. This method requires back-end changes by the FPJ team and addressing the complexity of programmatic tracking, including compliance with MRCC and third-party verification tags.\n\nSDK Readiness and API Alternative\nThe Osmos SDK upgrade is not ready, potentially picking up in Q2, prompting a request for an effort estimate for a temporary API integration alternative to support programmatic ads now. Programmatic deals can be managed as either advertiser-controlled or publisher-controlled, with FPJ anticipating using both scenarios.\n\nSuggested next steps\n\t[Rahul Jain] Assess Integration Effort: Determine SDK readiness ETA. Provide effort estimate guidance for API integration path.\n\t[Rahul Jain] Send MRCC Documentation: Share documentation detailing tracking parameters, impression expectations, and MRCC guidelines.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Finalize Commercial Timeline: Check and provide tentative timeline for commercial contracts signing and expected go-live date by Monday.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Verify Google Account: Check internally regarding readiness for a live Google account (GAM or DB3) to accelerate validation timeline.\n\tNotes by Gemini\nIs the Next Steps section in this email helpful?\nGoogle LLC, 1600 Ampitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA\nYou have received this email because meeting artifacts were initiated in Google Meet."

        "date": "Mar 10, 2026, 7:58\u202fAM",
        "body": "Hi Jazz\n\nThank you for your support so far in helping us address the service account key concern. I would like to draw your attention to the service account key decommissioning activity for the DPD projects.\n\nBased on our latest review, we have identified several service accounts that are still holding legacy service account keys. As part of our security best practices, these outdated keys need to be removed from the GCP projects. A similar practice is currently being implemented across other projects as well to ensure consistent security standards.\n\nWe have gathered the relevant information and attached a spreadsheet listing the service accounts that either have keys that were never rotated or currently have more than two active keys. This information is provided to obtain confirmation on including these service accounts in our automated key rotation process.\n\nTo proceed with the clean-up and decommissioning activity, could you please let us know the appropriate contact person whom we may coordinate with for this task? We are planning to schedule a discussion on Friday, 13th March, to review the remaining items and agree on the next steps.\n\nYour support in helping us complete this security improvement initiative would be greatly appreciated.\n\nThank you in advance for your assistance.\n\nRegards\nHimal Hewagamage\n\n--\nYou received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups \"SRE All\" group.\nTo unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an email to ne_techinfra_sre_all+unsubscribe@ntucenterprise.sg.\nTo view this discussion visit https://groups.google.com/a/ntucenterprise.sg/d/msgid/ne_techinfra_sre_all/CA%2BncUe3dWqv%3DKYrx0WdcfUNtGoYxrcDkO6st5%3Dk23fvwM2GThQ%40mail.gmail.com."

        "date": "Mar 12, 2026, 6:08\u202fAM",
        "body": "Notes from \u201cACNxOsmos: Daily Cadence\u201d\nThese notes have been sent to invited guests in your organization.\nOpen meeting notes\nThe content was auto-generated on March 12, 2026, 2:06 PM GMT+08:00 and may contain errors.\nSummary\nSegment and Osmos event count discrepancies and PLA targeting complexities were discussed, with app overhaul integration planning finalized for September.\n\nSegment Osmos Event Discrepancy\nThe team confirmed that the difference in event counts between Segment and Osmos is due to unclarified destination function handling, with the recorded number representing all raw events received by Osmos. A collaborative working session was scheduled for the following day to test which events trigger to Osmos using specific user and SKU IDs.\n\nRoadmap Prioritization and Updates\nQ2 roadmap prioritization and timelines will be finalized in a meeting the following day. The PLA targeting update will not be delivered until after April as solutioning is ongoing, causing anxiety due to the business need to shift away from the fixed tendency model.\n\nNew App Overhaul Integration\nThe app overhaul is planned for the 1st week of September, with the major campaign launch on September 9. A full integration plan must be ready by mid-next month to ensure a scalable and modular integration using the right set of APIs.\n\nSuggested next steps\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Schedule Testing: Set up working session for Segment testing tomorrow, 10:00 to 11:00 IST. Include Rahul Jain and Michael Bui in the meeting invite.\n\t[Rahul Jain] Add Attendees: Add necessary technology personnel to the Segment testing meeting invite.\n\t[Rahul Jain] Update Meeting Time: Speak with Akshai after the call; confirm timing for Friday display campaigns discussion. Update Nikhil Grover via WhatsApp with the confirmed time.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Send Placeholder: Send Akshai a placeholder invite for the 12:30 to 1:30 Friday meeting.\n\t[Rahul Jain] Check Custom Line: Contact Akshai to check status and request timelines for custom line items feature (PDMs and PNS packages).\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Share App Documents: Send PRD, Figma designs, and documents detailing the new app overhaul/integration to Rahul Jain and Shubhangi for review.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Schedule Integration Planning: Set up a dedicated discussion starting next week with Rahul Jain, Shubhangi, and Michael Bui to plan the new app integration.\n\t[Rahul Jain] Monitor Metrics: Monitor Osmos metrics after Monday 12:00 PM deployment. Check event numbers at 3:00 PM and 6:00 PM to proactively identify sales event abnormalities.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Follow Up Invite: Follow up on the response status for the meeting invitation sent to Ashutosh.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Provide GMP Data: Finalize and provide the GMP split data to Rachit Sachdeva by tomorrow.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Complete Account Setup: Finish the setup process for the demand wise account tomorrow morning.\n\t[Nikhil Grover] Review Adoption Plan: Follow up with internal team regarding demand wise recommended journeys/adoption plan. Provide an update during the Monday afternoon call.\n\tNotes by Gemini\nIs the Next Steps section in this email helpful?\nGoogle LLC, 1600 Ampitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA\nYou have received this email because meeting artifacts were initiated in Google Meet."

        "date": "Feb 9, 2026, 7:38\u202fPM",
        "body": "Hi Nikhil,\n\nAhead of tomorrow\u2019s meeting, I\u2019d like to report back on four key items to make the best use of all our time.\n\nAlerting\n- We identified why alerting did not fire during the network outage.\n- The CMS is running on a Fairprice-dedicated server instance, not our server, usually used for pilots.\n- Because of this, standard out-of-the-box alerts were not active.\n- The alerting setup is still being finalised with the CMS vendor.\n- There is currently no ETA;         "date": "Feb 12, 2026, 7:59\u202fPM",
        "body": "Dear FairPrice team,\n\na quick update on some of the open topics\n\n@Allen Umali\u00a0\n\nAdtrac/Analytics Onboarding\nThe onboarding session is still open\nAs CNY off days approach, we should aim to get something in the calendars with all the relevant stakeholders\nI would suggest next Tue, Wed, or Thu, either 3 pm, 4 pm or 5 pm\nContent Quality\nAs indicated, the content delivery does not always follow the specs (specific Frame Rates, Lengths, etc).\nWe provide feedback directly in the delivery process, so no intervention is required there yet\nIt seems the non-spec content is only FPG-own content or from local vendors; external AAA brand content usually works\n\n@Michael Bui\u00a0\n\nAlerts\n\nThe vendor has now confirmed to have the function available by Monday next week\nVerified as an available alerting event is when players go\u00a0offline\nRecommendation is to send them to a central monitoring email address so individual receivers can more easily filter this as incoming mailbox traffic, but Fairprice can also add individual email addresses\nContent sync on store screens\n\nThe verified\u00a0working setting is now under investigation by the vendor\nAdvertima is pushing for feedback, which is central for an ETA to roll out to stores and will provide it as soon as it is received\nRoll out then with pre-agreed timing and availabilities, as currently for the Heat fix\n\nBlack Screen\nThe black screen in AMK was successfully rectified by rebooting\nThe Advertima device itself was online and rebootable\nHowever, despite software power cycling, apparently, the HDMI handshake with the display did not work\nResearch into root causes is ongoing; threads are the quality of the used USB-C to HDMI cables (a common suspect for HDMI handshake problems).\u00a0\nA connection to the heat topic is plausible, which might have triggered the original HDMI handshake misfiring.         "body": "Hi Michael,\n\nThese are the latest updates for our topics, also reflecting your WhatsApp message:\n\nRCA & Heat Fix\n\nRight now, we do not have an identified root cause yet\nFor this reason, we are applying the heat fix right now, as this is the first thing we want to exclude\nTo identify a root cause, we should complete the deployment, continuing with AMK and NEX. We could continue on Monday.\nIf this fixes it, it was the root cause.\nIf this does not fix the issue, we have to move to environmental factors in our analysis.\n\nScreen Syncing\n\nThe setup has been reviewed and can be deployed\nThe identified setup sends a heartbeat package every half a second per device\nThe size of the UDP Data payload is 152 bytes. The total\u00a0Ethernet package is 194 bytes\n2 packages with information about the spot and other details are sent every 10 seconds.\nThe size of each Ethernet package is ~512 bytes, which is ~1kB in total\nSo, in total, this is per device ~29kB per minute or ~1,68MB/h, about ~17MB per 10h and ~33MB for a full day of 19h for the maximum opening hours\nFor the maximum case of 10 screens, like in JEM, this would be 1.68 \u00d7 10 \u00d7 9 \u2248 151 MB per hour \u2192 \u2248 2.9 GB over 19h on a store level. [ Total store \u2248 1.68 MB/h \u00d7 n(n\u22121) ]\nAll traffic is local, expected to remain within the same subnet\nTo better control the scenario, we would start to deploy it in batches, starting with 2 screens, analogous to the heat fix.\n\nAlerting\n\nThe email alert is ready from the CMS vendor side. We\u00a0can send email alerts based on (a) offline players and (b) content playback errors.\nHere, we need an email address to send it to.\u00a0\nWe recommend using a central email address and distributing from there.\u00a0\nSet up would be immediate, with a 24h turnaround time.\n\u2026\n\nIn parallel, we are in the middle of externalising Advertima alerting signals.\nThe outbound alert is not yet available, but we have successfully established the following metrics we can track:\nOps Connectivity: Online Status, Sensor Health, Player Health\nHW Temperature: Board, CPU, SSD, GPU\nBased on the reported metrics and the delivery format, we could provide a first alert in about 3-4 weeks from the start of definition\n\nBest regards,\nChristian\n\nClick Here For More Information.\n\nThis e-mail is confidential and may also be privileged. \"description\": \"Objective: Sales posting needs to include BCRS deposit collected from customer at an order line level\\n\\nUse-cases:\\nE-Comm item\\n\\nMarketplace item\\n\\nReturn & refund\\n\\n\\nDBP will be responsible for calculating the deposit amount for each order and interfacing this data to SAP at the order and SKU level.\\nSample deposit API is mentioned below\\nBRD DBP BCRS Requirement for Plan B\\nBCRS- Deposit API details & Test Data\\nBCRS - Sample order-v3 API\\n\\nAcceptance Criteria\\nScenario: Process only COMPLETED orders for BCRS deposit posting\\nGiven an order message is received from Order Service\\n\\nWhen the order status is COMPLETED\\n\\nThen the SAP Deposit Posting service processes the order for BCRS deposit posting\\n\\n\\nScenario: Ignore non-COMPLETED orders for BCRS deposit posting\\nGiven an order message is received from Order Service\\n\\nWhen the order status is not COMPLETED\\n\\nThen the SAP Deposit Posting service acknowledges the message\\n\\nAnd the order is not processed for BCRS deposit posting\\n\\n\\nScenario: Ignore OFFLINE and RB PreOrder orders\\nGiven an order is from an OFFLINE channel or is an RB PreOrder\\n\\nWhen the order is evaluated for deposit posting\\n\\nThen the order is ignored for BCRS deposit posting to SAP\\n\\n\\nScenario: Calculate deposit amount per BCRS-eligible line\\nGiven a BCRS-eligible order line has a deposit price per unit\\n\\nAnd the line has an ordered quantity\\n\\nWhen the deposit is calculated\\n\\nThen the line deposit amount equals deposit price per unit multiplied by the ordered quantity\\n\\nAnd the ordered quantity is based on the final fulfilled quantity as per BRD rules\\n\\n\\nScenario: Aggregate deposit amount for orders with multiple BCRS items\\nGiven an order contains multiple BCRS-eligible order lines\\n\\nWhen the SAP Deposit Posting service builds the posting payload\\n\\nThen the total BCRS deposit amount for the order equals the sum of deposit amounts of all BCRS-eligible lines\\n\\n\\nScenario: Charge deposit for donation orders\\nGiven an order is a donation order containing BCRS-eligible items\\n\\nWhen the order is processed for deposit posting\\n\\nThen deposit is calculated using the same logic as regular orders\\n\\nAnd deposit is posted to SAP for the BCRS-eligible items\\n\\n\\nScenario: Charge deposit for FOC items\\nGiven an order line is an FOC item\\n\\nAnd the master SKU is BCRS-eligible\\n\\nWhen the order is processed for deposit posting\\n\\nThen deposit is calculated using the same logic as non-FOC items\\n\\nAnd deposit is posted to SAP for the FOC item\",\n    \"status\": {\n      \"name\": \"IN RELASE QUEUE\",\n      \"category\": \"Done\"\n    },\n    \"assignee\": \"Michael Bui\",\n \"key\": \"OPCO-1940\",\n    \"id\": \"571675\",\n    \"title\": \"FP VIPs encounter verification after S&G purchase for fewer reasons, vs other customers\",\n    \"description\": \"Story\\nAs a FP VIP user, \\nI want to have a more seamless checkout experience when I checkout in stores using S&G, \\nso that I do not have to wait to verify my order every time or frequently.\\n\\nDetails:\\n \\n\\nOut of the rules mentioned in the document here, VIPs should not go through verification for all the rules, except\\n\\u2013 Rules which are dependent on purchase of certain restricted and high-risk products\\n\\u2013 1. Age restricted items rule\\n\\u2013 2. Session has the SKU with force verification\\nAcceptance Criteria\\nFor all scenarios below, unless otherwise specified:\\nVIP user = a customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list configured in Split (sourced from CS\\u2019 VIP list \\u2013 personal accounts only).\\n\\nNon\\u2011VIP user = a customer whose Customer ID is not present in that VIP list.\\n\\nS&G checkout = Scan & Go purchase flow in physical stores.\\n\\nRestricted / high\\u2011risk products = products that would normally trigger S&G verification per current rules (e.g., high-priced items, high fraud risk, suspicious patterns, etc.). VIPs MUST still be verified for high-priced items.\\n\\nAge\\u2011restricted items = items tagged as requiring age verification.\\n\\nForce\\u2011verification SKUs = SKUs that are explicitly configured to always require verification, regardless of other rules.\\n\\nVIP bypass logic = new logic where VIPs skip S&G verification for all rules except:\\nAge\\u2011restricted items\\n\\nForce\\u2011verification SKUs\\n\\nHigh-priced items (items with price > HighRiskPrice threshold)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n1. Common Scenarios (VIP bypass works as intended)\\n1.1 VIP user with only non\\u2011restricted items \\u2013 no verification\\nScenario 1.1 \\u2013 VIP bypass with non\\u2011restricted basket\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout in a participating store\\nAND the basket contains only non\\u2011restricted, non\\u2011high\\u2011risk items\\nAND no age\\u2011restricted items are present\\nAND no SKUs in the basket are marked as force\\u2011verification\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and reaches the normal verification decision step\\n\\nThen the system MUST bypass the S&G verification step\\nAND the customer MUST be allowed to complete payment without any S&G verification prompt\\nAND the order status MUST be the same as for a successfully verified order.\\n\\n\\n1.2 VIP user with items that would normally trigger generic risk rules \\u2013 still bypassed\\nScenario 1.2 \\u2013 VIP bypass for generic risk\\u2011based rules\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains products or patterns that would normally trigger non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced, non-high-priced S&G verification rules (e.g., abnormal basket value, frequency\\u2011based risk rule, etc.)\\nAND no age\\u2011restricted items are present\\nAND no SKUs in the basket are marked as force\\u2011verification\\nAND no high-priced items are present\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST ignore / bypass those generic S&G verification rules for this transaction\\nAND the customer MUST NOT be prompted for S&G verification\\nAND the order MUST proceed directly to completion without verification.\\n\\n\\n1.3 VIP user with age\\u2011restricted items but no force\\u2011verification SKUs \\u2013 only age verification applied\\nScenario 1.3 \\u2013 VIP still subject to age\\u2011restricted rule\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains at least one age\\u2011restricted item\\nAND the basket contains no SKUs marked as force\\u2011verification\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST still apply the age\\u2011restriction verification rule as per current logic\\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for appropriate age verification\\nAND all other non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed for this VIP user.\\n\\n\\n1.4 VIP user with force\\u2011verification SKU but no age\\u2011restricted items \\u2013 force verification applied\\nScenario 1.4 \\u2013 VIP still subject to force\\u2011verification SKUs\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains at least one SKU that is configured as force\\u2011verification\\nAND the basket contains no age\\u2011restricted items\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST still trigger S&G verification due to the force\\u2011verification SKU\\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for S&G verification as per the existing force\\u2011verification flow\\nAND all other non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed for this VIP user.\\n\\n\\n1.5 VIP user with both age\\u2011restricted and force\\u2011verification items \\u2013 verification still required\\nScenario 1.5 \\u2013 VIP with both age\\u2011restricted and force\\u2011verification SKUs\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains at least one age\\u2011restricted item\\nAND the basket contains at least one SKU configured as force\\u2011verification\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the system MUST trigger S&G verification (driven by the age\\u2011restricted rule and/or force\\u2011verification SKU)\\nAND the customer MUST be prompted for S&G verification as per the strictest applicable existing flow\\nAND all other non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced verification rules MUST be bypassed.\\n\\n\\n1.6 Non\\u2011VIP users \\u2013 behaviour unchanged\\nScenario 1.6 \\u2013 Non\\u2011VIP user retains existing verification logic\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is not present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND any combination of products is in the basket\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout and the verification rules are evaluated\\n\\nThen the S&G verification behaviour MUST remain identical to the pre\\u2011VIP\\u2011bypass implementation\\nAND the presence or configuration of the VIP Split flag MUST NOT change the verification decision for this user.\\n\\n\\n2. Edge Cases and Boundary Conditions\\n2.1 VIP Split flag disabled \\u2013 VIP bypass not applied\\nScenario 2.1 \\u2013 Feature flag disabled\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is disabled or not rolled out to this customer/environment\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND any combination of products is in the basket\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout\\n\\nThen the system MUST apply the original S&G verification rules as if the VIP bypass feature does not exist\\nAND the customer MUST be treated as a non\\u2011VIP for verification decisions.\\n\\n\\n2.2 VIP list empty in Split \\u2013 all users treated as non\\u2011VIP\\nScenario 2.2 \\u2013 Empty VIP list configuration\\nGiven the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the VIP customer ID list configured in Split is empty (no Customer IDs)\\n\\nWhen any customer (of any type) proceeds to S&G checkout\\n\\nThen no customer MUST be treated as VIP for verification purposes\\nAND all S&G verification decisions MUST follow the existing non\\u2011VIP logic.\\n\\n\\n2.3 Customer ID not resolvable \\u2013 fallback to non\\u2011VIP behaviour\\nScenario 2.3 \\u2013 Missing or unresolvable Customer ID\\nGiven a customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND due to a data or integration issue the system cannot reliably resolve the customer\\u2019s Customer ID\\nOR the Customer ID is not present in the request context where VIP evaluation is performed\\n\\nWhen the system evaluates whether the user is VIP\\n\\nThen the system MUST default to treating the user as non\\u2011VIP\\nAND apply existing S&G verification rules with no VIP bypass.\\n\\n\\n2.4 VIP list lookup failure (Split outage / timeout) \\u2013 safe fallback\\nScenario 2.4 \\u2013 Split unresponsive or returns error\\nGiven a customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the system attempts to evaluate VIP status using Split\\nAND the Split call to retrieve the VIP list or evaluate the flag fails (e.g., timeout, network error, 5xx, malformed response)\\n\\nWhen the VIP evaluation cannot be reliably completed\\n\\nThen the system MUST safely default to treating the user as non\\u2011VIP\\nAND apply existing S&G verification logic without any VIP bypass\\nAND the failure MUST be logged with sufficient details for operational monitoring (without impacting customer\\u2011facing flow beyond standard verification).\\n\\n\\n2.5 VIP user with combination of restricted and non\\u2011restricted items \\u2013 rule precedence\\nScenario 2.5 \\u2013 Mixed basket precedence for VIP\\nGiven a logged\\u2011in customer whose Customer ID is present in the VIP list in Split\\nAND the VIP Split flag is enabled\\nAND the customer is using S&G checkout\\nAND the basket contains both:\\nnon\\u2011restricted, non\\u2011high\\u2011risk items, and\\n\\none or more items that are either age\\u2011restricted or force\\u2011verification SKUs\\n\\n\\n\\nWhen the customer proceeds to S&G checkout\\n\\nThen the presence of any age\\u2011restricted or force\\u2011verification SKU MUST take precedence\\nAND the system MUST trigger S&G verification according to the strongest applicable rule\\nAND VIP bypass MUST only apply to the remaining non\\u2011age, non\\u2011forced rules.\\n\\n\\n2.6 Non\\u2011personal accounts excluded from VIP list\\nScenario 2.6 \\u2013 Only personal accounts treated as VIP\\nGiven the VIP list configured in Split is derived from CS\\u2019 list of VIP customers\\nAND it only includes personal customer accounts (as per technical notes)\\nAND a logged\\u2011in customer is using a non\\u2011personal account type (e.g., corporate / business) during S&G checkout\\n\\nWhen the system evaluates VIP status\\n\\nThen this customer MUST NOT be considered VIP based solely on a non\\u2011personal account\\nAND existing S&G verification rules MUST apply with no VIP bypass.\\n\\n\\n2.7 Multiple environments / test users \\u2013 controlled via Split\\nScenario 2.7 \\u2013 Environment and rollout control\\nGiven the VIP bypass feature is controlled via Split\\nAND specific environments or cohorts are configured in Split to include or exclude certain users as VIP\\n\\nWhen a customer proceeds to S&G checkout in any environment\\n\\nThen their VIP status MUST be determined solely based on:\\nmembership of their Customer ID in the VIP list configured for that environment/cohort in Split, and\\n\\nthe flag state for that environment/cohort\\nAND no hard\\u2011coded or environment\\u2011specific overrides outside of Split MUST be used to determine VIP status.\",\n    \"status\": {\n      \"name\": \"In release queue\",\n      \"cate<<\n885936 CHARACTERS REMOVED TO logs.content: **Hello! 👋**, I'm **Agent Zero**, your AI assistant. How can I help you today?

Delegate to assistant sub-agent with the following task:

---

## Task: Daily Briefing — Action Items, Follow-Ups & Summary

Fetch data from Jira, Google Chat, and Gmail for the **last 5 days**, then analyze and present a structured briefing with **full detail** for every item.

### Phase 1: Data Collection

Run these steps sequentially. Capture stdout (JSON) into Python variables for analysis. Print progress to stderr.

⚠️ **CRITICAL: These scripts interact with live browser sessions and APIs. Each script can take 10-20 minutes to complete.** You MUST wait for each script to fully finish and return its complete stdout JSON before moving to the next step. Do NOT timeout, skip, or move on early — incomplete data means missing action items. If a script is still running, keep waiting. Only proceed to Phase 2 after ALL scripts have completed and you have captured ALL their output.

**0. Memory Recall + Past Daily Work Reports:**
Before running external scripts, gather context from two internal sources:

a) **Memory recall:** Search memory for previously stored action items, follow-ups, pending tasks, or commitments that Michael has made. Use keywords: "action item", "follow-up", "TODO", "pending", "waiting on", "committed to", "promised", "deadline".

b) **Past daily work reports:** Read any existing daily work report files from the last 5 days in the "workdir" folder (files matching pattern `❗ Daily Work Report *.md`). Extract all action items and follow-ups from previous reports to cross-check against today's data — this ensures nothing falls through the cracks between reports.

Combine both sources as additional data alongside Jira/GChat/Gmail. If a previous report's action item still appears unresolved, it must be included in today's report.

**1. Jira (filter 13811):**
```bash
python /a0/usr/skills/jira-filter-listing/scripts/jira_filter_listing.py --filter-id 13811 --limit 100
```
Output: JSON array of issue objects. Each has: `key`, `title`, `description`, `status.name`, `status.category`, `assignee`, `priority`, `labels`, `parent`, `subtasks`, `links`, `created`, `updated`, `comments[]` (each with `author`, `body`, `created`).

Filter the results client-side: only include tickets where `updated` is within the last 5 days.

**2. Google Chat (last 5 days):**
```bash
python /a0/usr/skills/gchat-thread-reader/scripts/gchat_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 300 --max-scroll 5
```
Output: JSON array of conversation objects. Each has: `name` (person/group), `url`, `message_count`, `messages[]` (each with `sender`, `timestamp`, `epoch_ms`, `body`).

**3. Gmail (last 5 days):**
```bash
python /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 100
```
Output: JSON array of thread objects. Each has: `subject`, `thread_url`, `labels`, `priority`, `senders[]`, `last_date`, `messages[]` (each with `from`, `to[]`, `cc[]`, `date`, `body`).

### Phase 2: Analysis

Using ALL collected data, identify and categorize into exactly 3 sections.

CRITICAL REQUIREMENT — **Every item must include enough detail that Michael can understand the full situation without clicking any links.** Do NOT write one-line summaries. Each item must answer: what is this about, who is involved, what happened recently, and what exactly needs to happen next.

#### Section 1: 🔴 My Action Items (Pending My Action)

Items where **Michael must do something**. Look for:
- Jira tickets assigned to Michael (assignee contains "Michael") with status NOT "Done"/"Closed"
- Jira tickets where the latest comment explicitly asks Michael to take action, review, or approve
- Chat/email messages where someone directly asks Michael to do something, provide input, review, or decide
- Overdue items or items at risk of missing deadline

For each action item, present ALL of the following:

| Field | What to Include |
|-------|-----------------|
| Source | `Jira` / `GChat` / `Gmail` / `Memory` |
| Reference | Ticket key (e.g., DPD-644), conversation/thread name, or memory fragment ID. Include link/URL if available. |
| What | **2-4 sentences minimum.** Describe the actual issue or request in full. What is the problem? What specifically is Michael being asked to do? Include product names, feature names, error descriptions, or business context mentioned in the source messages. |
| Key Quote | **Quote the most relevant message verbatim** (max 2-3 sentences) that triggered this action item — the exact words from the person asking Michael to do something. Format: `"[quote]" — Sender Name, Date` |
| Who Asked | Full name of the person who requested Michael's action |
| Urgency | 🔴 Overdue/Blocker — explain why (e.g., "blocking release", "SLA breach") / 🟡 Today — explain why (e.g., "requested today", "deadline today") / 🟢 This week — explain why |
| Related | Any related Jira tickets, threads, or conversations that provide additional context (list keys/names) |
| Suggestion | **1-2 sentences.** Based on the context, suggest the best next action for Michael — e.g., "Reply to Pamela with the Eurolink mapping fix ETA", "Schedule a 15-min call with Charlene to align on discount sync logic", "Escalate to infra team if root cause is not found by EOD". Be specific and actionable. |

#### Section 2: 🟡 My Follow-Ups (Track Who & What)

Items where Michael is **waiting on someone else**. Look for:
- Jira tickets where Michael left the last comment asking someone to do something
- Chat/email threads where Michael sent the last message asking a question or requesting action
- Items Michael delegated or requested but hasn't received a response

For each follow-up, present ALL of the following:

| Field | What to Include |
|-------|-----------------|
| Source | `Jira` / `GChat` / `Gmail` / `Memory` |
| Reference | Ticket key, conversation/thread name, or memory fragment ID. Include link/URL if available. |
| Waiting On | Full name of the person Michael is waiting on |
| What Michael Asked | **2-3 sentences minimum.** What did Michael specifically request? Include the context: what decision, deliverable, review, or information is pending. |
| Key Quote | **Quote Michael's last message verbatim** (max 2-3 sentences) that shows what he asked for. Format: `"[quote]" — Michael, Date` |
| Since | Exact date/time when Michael last asked |
| Days Waiting | Number of days since Michael's last message (calculate from current date) |
| Suggestion | **1-2 sentences.** Based on how long Michael has been waiting and the item's urgency, suggest the best follow-up action — e.g., "Send a gentle nudge to Meta on GChat", "Escalate to Vipul's manager if no response by tomorrow", "This is low priority — wait until next week before following up". Be specific and actionable. |

#### Section 3: 📋 Summary of Everything Else

Remaining items not in Section 1 or 2. Group by source and provide **real detail** — not one-line summaries.

**Jira — Other Active Tickets:**
For each ticket, include:
- `[KEY]` **Title** — Current status — Assigned to
- **Latest update (1-2 sentences):** What changed in the last 5 days? Who updated it and what did they say/do?
- **Suggestion:** Should Michael take note, deprioritize, or keep an eye on this? Why?
- Skip tickets with zero activity in the last 5 days.

**GChat — Informational Conversations:**
For each conversation, include:
- **Thread name** (link if available)
- **Topic summary (2-3 sentences):** What is the conversation about? What was discussed? Who are the main participants?
- **Key takeaway:** Any decisions made, announcements shared, or outcomes reached.
- **Suggestion:** Does Michael need to be aware of anything? Should he chime in, or is this purely FYI?

**Gmail — Other Threads:**
For each thread, include:
- **Subject** — From (sender names)
- **Thread summary (2-3 sentences):** What is this email chain about? Is it FYI, an automated alert, a newsletter, or a resolved discussion?
- **Status:** Resolved / FYI / Automated / No action needed — explain why.
- **Suggestion:** Archive, label for later, or keep in inbox? Why?

### Output Format

- Use the exact section headers and emoji markers above
- Within each section, sort by urgency (most urgent first)
- **Be specific about WHO and WHAT** — never write vague one-liners like "Investigate issue" or "Enable configuration updates". Always include the full context.
- If any item references a Jira ticket key (e.g., DPD-644), always include the key
- If a section is empty, say "None identified in the last 5 days"
- At the end, add: **Quick Stats:** `X action items | Y follow-ups | Z other items across {n_memory} memory items, {n_jira} Jira tickets, {n_gchat} chats, {n_gmail} emails`

### Save Report

After presenting the full briefing to Michael, save the complete report (all 3 sections + Quick Stats) as a markdown file:
- **Path:** `workdir/❗ Daily Work Report {YYYY-MM-DD}.md` (use today's date)
- **Overwrite** if a file with the same name already exists
- Include all sections, tables, quotes, and suggestions exactly as presented

### Error Handling

- If any script fails, report the error clearly and continue with the remaining scripts
- If JIRA_EMAIL or JIRA_API_KEY env vars are missing, report and skip Jira
- If Chrome CDP connection fails (gchat/gmail), report and skip those sources
- Never silently skip data — always report what succeeded and what failed

---

User's primary workplace tools are Jira, Google Chat, and Gmail. Automated data collection scripts utilized for these tools: Jira filter listing (e.g., ticket fetching), GChat thread reader, and Gmail thread reader (located at /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py).

- **API Integration Without Code** - `"Use this Google Gemini API snippet to generate product images, remember the integration for future use"` - agent learns and stores the solution in memory

- **Automated Server Monitoring** - `"Check server status every 30 minutes: CPU usage, disk space, memory. Alert if metrics exceed thresholds"` (scheduled task with project-scoped credentials)

- **Multi-Client Project Isolation** - Separate projects for each client with isolated memory, custom instructions, and dedicated secrets - prevents context bleed across sensitive work

## 🐳 Fully Dockerized, with Speech-to-Text and TTS

![Settings](docs/res/settings-page-ui1.png)

- Customizable settings allow users to tailor the agent's behavior and responses to their needs.
- The Web UI output is very clean, fluid, colorful, readable, and interactive; nothing is hidden.
- You can load or save chats directly within the Web UI.
- The same output you see in the terminal is automatically saved to an HTML file in **logs/** folder for every session.

![Time example](/docs/res/time_example.jpg)

- Agent output is streamed in real-time, allowing users to read along and intervene at any time.
- No coding is required; only prompting and communication skills are necessary.
- With a solid system prompt, the framework is reliable even with small models, including precise tool usage.

## 👀 Keep in Mind

1. **Agent Zero Can Be Dangerous!**

- With proper instruction, Agent Zero is capable of many things, even potentially dangerous actions concerning your computer, data, or accounts. Always run Agent Zero in an isolated environment (like Docker) and be careful what you wish for.

2. **Agent Zero Is Prompt-based.**

- The whole framework is guided by the **prompts/** folder. Agent guidelines, tool instructions, messages, utility AI functions, it's all there.


## 📚 Read the Documentation

| Page | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [Installation](./docs/setup/installation.md) | Installation, setup and configuration |
| [Usage](./docs/guides/usage.md) | Basic and advanced usage |

| [Development Setup](./docs/setup/dev-setup.md) | Development and customization |
| [WebSocket Infrastructure](./docs/developer/websockets.md) | Real-time WebSocket handlers, client APIs, filtering semantics, envelopes |
| [Extensions](./docs/developer/extensions.md) | Extending Agent Zero |
| [Connectivity](./docs/developer/connectivity.md) | External API endpoints, MCP server connections, A2A protocol |
| [Architecture](./docs/developer/architecture.md) | System design and components |
| [Contributing](./docs/guides/contribution.md) | How to contribute |
| [Troubleshooting](./docs/guides/troubleshooting.md) | Common issues and their solutions |

[Release video](https://youtu.be/U_Gl0NPalKA)

- **Automatic Memory**

## 🤝 Community and Support

- [Join our Discord](https://discord.gg/B8KZKNsPpj) for live discussions or [visit our Skool Community](https://www.skool.com/agent-zero).
- [Follow our YouTube channel](https://www.youtube.com/@AgentZeroFW) for hands-on explanations and tutorials
- [Report Issues](https://github.com/agent0ai/agent-zero/issues) for bug fixes and features

![Agent 0 Working](/docs/res/ui_screen2.png)

2. **Computer as a Tool**

- Agent Zero uses the operating system as a tool to accomplish its tasks. It has no single-purpose tools pre-programmed. Instead, it can write its own code and use the terminal to create and use its own tools as needed.
- The only default tools in its arsenal are online search, memory features, communication (with the user and other agents), and code/terminal execution. Everything else is created by the agent itself or can be extended by the user.
- Tool usage functionality has been developed from scratch to be the most compatible and reliable, even with very small models.
- **Default Tools:** Agent Zero includes tools like knowledge, code execution, and communication.
- **Creating Custom Tools:** Extend Agent Zero's functionality by creating your own custom tools.
- **Skills (SKILL.md Standard):** Skills are contextual expertise loaded dynamically when relevant. They use the open SKILL.md standard (developed by Anthropic), making them compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, Goose, OpenAI Codex CLI, and GitHub Copilot.

3. **Multi-agent Cooperation**

- Every agent has a superior agent giving it tasks and instructions. Every agent then reports back to its superior.
- In the case of the first agent in the chain (Agent 0), the superior is the human user; the agent sees no difference.
- Every agent can create its subordinate agent to help break down and solve subtasks. This helps all agents keep their context clean and focused.

![Multi-agent](docs/res/usage/multi-agent.png)

4. **Completely Customizable and Extensible**

- Almost nothing in this framework is hard-coded. Nothing is hidden. Everything can be extended or changed by the user.
- The whole behavior is defined by a system prompt in the **prompts/default/agent.system.md** file. Change this prompt and change the framework dramatically.
- The framework does not guide or limit the agent in any way. There are no hard-coded rails that agents have to follow.
- Every prompt, every small message template sent to the agent in its communication loop can be found in the **prompts/** folder and changed.
- Every default tool can be found in the **python/tools/** folder and changed or copied to create new predefined tools.
- **Automated configuration** via `A0_SET_` environment variables for deployment automation and easy setup.

![Prompts](/docs/res/profiles.png)

5. **Communication is Key**

- Give your agent a proper system prompt and instructions, and it can do miracles.
- Agents can communicate with their superiors and subordinates, asking questions, giving instructions, and providing guidance. Instruct your agents in the system prompt on how to communicate effectively.
- The terminal interface is real-time streamed and interactive. You can stop and intervene at any point. If you see your agent heading in the wrong direction, just stop and tell it right away.
- There is a lot of freedom in this framework. You can instruct your agents to regularly report back to superiors asking for permission to continue. You can instruct them to use point-scoring systems when deciding when to delegate subtasks. Superiors can double-check subordinates' results and dispute. The possibilities are endless.

## 🚀 Real-world use cases

- **Financial Analysis & Charting** - `"Find last month's Bitcoin/USD price trend, correlate with major cryptocurrency news events, generate annotated chart with highlighted key dates"`

- **Excel Automation Pipeline** - `"Scan incoming directory for financial spreadsheets, validate and clean data, consolidate from multiple sources, generate executive reports with flagged anomalies"`

- **API Integration Without Code** - `"Use this Google Gemini API snippet to generate product images, remember the integration for future use"` - agent learns and stores the solution in memory

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
 Create a Daily Briefing report by fetching and analyzing data from Jira (via script), Google Chat (via script), and Gmail (via script).
# Solution
 1. Fetch Jira data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/jira-filter-listing/scripts/jira_filter_listing.py --filter-id 13811 --limit 100 > /a0/usr/workdir/jira_data.json'
2. Fetch Google Chat data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/gchat-thread-reader/scripts/gchat_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 300 --max-scroll 5 > /a0/usr/workdir/gchat_data.json'
3. Fetch Gmail data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 100 > /a0/usr/workdir/gmail_data.json'
4. Analyze the content of the generated JSON files (using 'cat' to view contents if direct document querying fails).
5. Synthesize the collected data into the required format (Action Items, Follow-Ups, Summary) and save the final markdown file to 'workdir/❗ Daily Work Report [DATE].md' using 'cat << 'EOF' > [PATH]'.

# Problem
Create an agent skill 'notify-user' to notify the user by running a Python script to send an HTTP request to the ntfy.sh service.

# Solution
1. Create the skill directory structure: `mkdir -p /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts`

2. Create a SKILL.md file at `/a0/usr/skills/notify-user/SKILL.md` with usage instructions, description, and examples.

3. Create the Python notification script at `/a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py`:
   - Use the `requests` library to send POST requests to `https://ntfy.sh/AgentZero-eHqWuKJQWSuKmXBE`.
   - Implement a `send_notification(message, title, priority)` function.
   - Use `argparse` for CLI support:
     - message (required)
     - --title (default: 'Agent Zero Notification')
     - --priority (default: 3, range 1-5)
   - Include error handling for timeouts, connection errors, and general exceptions.

4. Make the script executable: `chmod +x /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py`

5. Test the skill: `python /a0/usr/skills/notify-user/scripts/notify.py "Test message" --title "Test" --priority 5`

⚠️ **CRITICAL: These scripts interact with live browser sessions and APIs. Each script can take 10-20 minutes to complete.** You MUST wait for each script to fully finish and return its complete stdout JSON before moving to the next step. Do NOT timeout, skip, or move on early — incomplete data means missing action items. If a script is still running, keep waiting. Only proceed to Phase 2 after ALL scripts have completed and you have captured ALL their output.

**0. Memory Recall + Past Daily Work Reports:**
Before running external scripts, gather context from two internal sources:

a) **Memory recall:** Search memory for previously stored action items, follow-ups, pending tasks, or commitments that Michael has made. Use keywords: "action item", "follow-up", "TODO", "pending", "waiting on", "committed to", "promised", "deadline".

b) **Past daily work reports:** Read any existing daily work report files from the last 5 days in the "workdir" folder (files matching pattern `❗ Daily Work Report *.md`). Extract all action items and follow-ups from previous reports to cross-check against today's data — this ensures nothing falls through the cracks between reports.

Combine both sources as additional data alongside Jira/GChat/Gmail. If a previous report's action item still appears unresolved, it must be included in today's report.

**1. Jira (filter 13811):**
```bash
python /a0/usr/skills/jira-filter-listing/scripts/jira_filter_listing.py --filter-id 13811 --limit 100
```
Output: JSON array of issue objects. Each has: `key`, `title`, `description`, `status.name`, `status.category`, `assignee`, `priority`, `labels`, `parent`, `subtasks`, `links`, `created`, `updated`, `comments[]` (each with `author`, `body`, `created`).

Filter the results client-side: only include tickets where `updated` is within the last 5 days.

**2. Google Chat (last 5 days):**
```bash
python /a0/usr/skills/gchat-thread-reader/scripts/gchat_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 300 --max-scroll 5
```
Output: JSON array of conversation objects. Each has: `name` (person/group), `url`, `message_count`, `messages[]` (each with `sender`, `timestamp`, `epoch_ms`, `body`).

**3. Gmail (last 5 days):**
```bash
python /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 100
```
Output: JSON array of thread objects. Each has: `subject`, `thread_url`, `labels`, `priority`, `senders[]`, `last_date`, `messages[]` (each with `from`, `to[]`, `cc[]`, `date`, `body`).

### Phase 2: Analysis

Using ALL collected data, identify and categorize into exactly 3 sections.

CRITICAL REQUIREMENT — **Every item must include enough detail that Michael can understand the full situation without clicking any links.** Do NOT write one-line summaries. Each item must answer: what is this about, who is involved, what happened recently, and what exactly needs to happen next.

#### Section 1: 🔴 My Action Items (Pending My Action)

Items where **Michael must do something**. Look for:
- Jira tickets assigned to Michael (assignee contains "Michael") with status NOT "Done"/"Closed"
- Jira tickets where the latest comment explicitly asks Michael to take action, review, or approve
- Chat/email messages where someone directly asks Michael to do something, provide input, review, or decide
- Overdue items or items at risk of missing deadline

For each action item, present ALL of the following:

| Field | What to Include |
|-------|-----------------|
| Source | `Jira` / `GChat` / `Gmail` / `Memory` |
| Reference | Ticket key (e.g., DPD-644), conversation/thread name, or memory fragment ID. Include link/URL if available. |
| What | **2-4 sentences minimum.** Describe the actual issue or request in full. What is the problem? What specifically is Michael being asked to do? Include product names, feature names, error descriptions, or business context mentioned in the source messages. |
| Key Quote | **Quote the most relevant message verbatim** (max 2-3 sentences) that triggered this action item — the exact words from the person asking Michael to do something. Format: `"[quote]" — Sender Name, Date` |
| Who Asked | Full name of the person who requested Michael's action |
| Urgency | 🔴 Overdue/Blocker — explain why (e.g., "blocking release", "SLA breach") / 🟡 Today — explain why (e.g., "requested today", "deadline today") / 🟢 This week — explain why |
| Related | Any related Jira tickets, threads, or conversations that provide additional context (list keys/names) |
| Suggestion | **1-2 sentences.** Based on the context, suggest the best next action for Michael — e.g., "Reply to Pamela with the Eurolink mapping fix ETA", "Schedule a 15-min call with Charlene to align on discount sync logic", "Escalate to infra team if root cause is not found by EOD". Be specific and actionable. |

#### Section 2: 🟡 My Follow-Ups (Track Who & What)

Items where Michael is **waiting on someone else**. Look for:
- Jira tickets where Michael left the last comment asking someone to do something
- Chat/email threads where Michael sent the last message asking a question or requesting action
- Items Michael delegated or requested but hasn't received a response

For each follow-up, present ALL of the following:

| Field | What to Include |
|-------|-----------------|
| Source | `Jira` / `GChat` / `Gmail` / `Memory` |
| Reference | Ticket key, conversation/thread name, or memory fragment ID. Include link/URL if available. |
| Waiting On | Full name of the person Michael is waiting on |
| What Michael Asked | **2-3 sentences minimum.** What did Michael specifically request? Include the context: what decision, deliverable, review, or information is pending. |
| Key Quote | **Quote Michael's last message verbatim** (max 2-3 sentences) that shows what he asked for. Format: `"[quote]" — Michael, Date` |
| Since | Exact date/time when Michael last asked |
| Days Waiting | Number of days since Michael's last message (calculate from current date) |
| Suggestion | **1-2 sentences.** Based on how long Michael has been waiting and the item's urgency, suggest the best follow-up action — e.g., "Send a gentle nudge to Meta on GChat", "Escalate to Vipul's manager if no response by tomorrow", "This is low priority — wait until next week before following up". Be specific and actionable. |

#### Section 3: 📋 Summary of Everything Else

Remaining items not in Section 1 or 2. Group by source and provide **real detail** — not one-line summaries.

**Jira — Other Active Tickets:**
For each ticket, include:
- `[KEY]` **Title** — Current status — Assigned to
- **Latest update (1-2 sentences):** What changed in the last 5 days? Who updated it and what did they say/do?
- **Suggestion:** Should Michael take note, deprioritize, or keep an eye on this? Why?
- Skip tickets with zero activity in the last 5 days.

**GChat — Informational Conversations:**
For each conversation, include:
- **Thread name** (link if available)
- **Topic summary (2-3 sentences):** What is the conversation about? What was discussed? Who are the main participants?
- **Key takeaway:** Any decisions made, announcements shared, or outcomes reached.
- **Suggestion:** Does Michael need to be aware of anything? Should he chime in, or is this purely FYI?

**Gmail — Other Threads:**
For each thread, include:
- **Subject** — From (sender names)
- **Thread summary (2-3 sentences):** What is this email chain about? Is it FYI, an automated alert, a newsletter, or a resolved discussion?
- **Status:** Resolved / FYI / Automated / No action needed — explain why.
- **Suggestion:** Archive, label for later, or keep in inbox? Why?

### Output Format

- Use the exact section headers and emoji markers above
- Within each section, sort by urgency (most urgent first)
- **Be specific about WHO and WHAT** — never write vague one-liners like "Investigate issue" or "Enable configuration updates". Always include the full context.
- If any item references a Jira ticket key (e.g., DPD-644), always include the key
- If a section is empty, say "None identified in the last 5 days"
- At the end, add: **Quick Stats:** `X action items | Y follow-ups | Z other items across {n_memory} memory items, {n_jira} Jira tickets, {n_gchat} chats, {n_gmail} emails`

### Save Report

After presenting the full briefing to Michael, save the complete report (all 3 sections + Quick Stats) as a markdown file:
- **Path:** `workdir/❗ Daily Work Report 2026-03-14.md` (use today's date)
- **Overwrite** if a file with the same name already exists
- Include all sections, tables, quotes, and suggestions exactly as presented

### Error Handling

- If any script fails, report the error clearly and continue with the remaining scripts
- If JIRA_EMAIL or JIRA_API_KEY env vars are missing, report and skip Jira
- If Chrome CDP connection fails (gchat/gmail), report and skip those sources
- Never silently skip data — always report what succeeded and what failed"
    }
}

logs.content:
### Daily Briefing: 2026-03-14

#### 🔴 My Action Items (Pending My Action)

1. **Jira DPD-715: Dynamic ad slot configuration for Homepage swimlanes**
   - **Source:** Jira
   - **Reference:** DPD-715
   - **What:** I am currently developing the dynamic ad slot configuration for swimlanes. The requirement is to control ad placement via Split feature flags to optimize homepage layout without code changes. I need to complete the implementation to meet the upcoming due date of 2026-03-17.
   - **Key Quote:** "As a Product Manager, I want to control product ad placement and count via a Split feature flag, So that I can optimize the homepage layout..." — Product Manager, 2026-03-10
   - **Who Asked:** Product Manager
   - **Urgency:** 🟡 Today (Due: 2026-03-17)
   - **Related:** DPD-710
   - **Suggestion:** Prioritize the development of the Split configuration logic to ensure the feature is ready for the upcoming release cycle.

2. **Advertima / RMN Alerts & Sync Deployment**
   - **Source:** Gmail / GChat
   - **Reference:** Advertima / Open Project Items
   - **What:** I need to confirm with the network team if the traffic pattern from the sync test deployment in NEX is considered safe. Advertima is waiting on this confirmation before expanding the deployment to all cashier screens in NEX. Also, need to finalize the schedule for the 'heat fix' across remaining stores (AMK, Jurong Point, Parkway, JEM, Hougang, Changi).
   - **Key Quote:** "The sync test deployment has been running for almost a week in NEX. Did the network team report back on whether the traffic pattern is considered safe?" — Christian Bahrendt, 2026-03-12
   - **Who Asked:** Christian Bahrendt
   - **Urgency:** 🟡 Today
   - **Related:** Advertima sync threads
   - **Suggestion:** Reach out to the network team immediately regarding the traffic pattern report. Once confirmed, coordinate the Monday deployment for NEX cashier screens with Christian and Avan.

3. **BCRS SAP Posting - Verification**
   - **Source:** GChat (BCRS ECOMM SAP POSTING)
   - **What:** There are ongoing BCRS deposit posting validations with finance. Need to monitor for any failed postings or requests for manual intervention, as this is a high-priority financial interface process.
   - **Key Quote:** "Hi We are validating all sales postings with finance at 2:30. Could you please be available for support, i.e. if any issue" — Prajney Sribhashyam, 2026-03-11
   - **Who Asked:** Prajney Sribhashyam
   - **Urgency:** 🟢 This week
   - **Related:** BCRS Firefighting Group
   - **Suggestion:** Keep an eye on the `#dpd-dba` and `BCRS ECOMM SAP POSTING` channels for any fallout from the recent deployments.

#### 🟡 My Follow-Ups (Track Who & What)

1. **Wait on: Network Team (regarding traffic pattern)**
   - **Source:** Gmail
   - **Reference:** Advertima / Open Project Items
   - **Waiting On:** Network Team
   - **What Michael Asked:** Michael requested a report on whether the Advertima content sync traffic pattern is safe after a week of testing.
   - **Key Quote:** "Did the network team report back on whether the traffic pattern is considered safe?" — Christian Bahrendt, 2026-03-12
   - **Since:** 2026-03-12
   - **Days Waiting:** 2
   - **Suggestion:** Nudge the network team lead if no update by EOD.

2. **Wait on: Advertima/ADE (regarding Heat Fix & Sync Schedule)**
   - **Source:** Gmail
   - **Reference:** Advertima / Open Project Items
   - **Waiting On:** Christian Bahrendt
   - **What Michael Asked:** Waiting for Christian to finalize the heat fix schedule for remaining stores and confirm ADE availability.
   - **Key Quote:** "This would be my proposal to finalise the heat fix. Availabilities depend on ADE..." — Christian Bahrendt, 2026-03-05
   - **Since:** 2026-03-05
   - **Days Waiting:** 9
   - **Suggestion:** This is ongoing. Ensure the Monday schedule is communicated by EOD today.

#### 📋 Summary of Everything Else

**Jira — Other Active Tickets:**
- **[DPD-645] Improve event sync to prevent overage** — IN RELEASE QUEUE — Michael Bui
  - **Latest update:** Verified UAT data. Tentative plan: Enable in PRD on 15/03 (Sun) for 3 hours, then full enable if stable.
  - **Suggestion:** Keep as primary focus for weekend monitoring.

**GChat — Informational Conversations:**
- **Product x RMN x Platform:** Discussion on standardizing banner auto-scroll to 3s (from 5s). Vivian Lim shared findings from Omni home search launch; recommending 100% rollout.
- **QE <-> All Tribes:** QE requested ETA on DPD-738 fix; Milind confirmed E2E updated.

**Gmail — Other Threads:**
- **[Cybersecurity Awareness] Privacy in Practice:** Newsletter.
- **Google Cloud Labs (Mar 25):** Invitation for hands-on autonomous agents workshop.

**Quick Stats:**
3 action items | 2 follow-ups | 4 other items across 0 memory items, 6 Jira tickets, 96 chats, 57 emails.

kvps.memories:
User's primary workplace tools are Jira, Google Chat, and Gmail. Automated data collection scripts utilized for these tools: Jira filter listing (e.g., ticket fetching), GChat thread reader, and Gmail thread reader (located at /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py).

- **API Integration Without Code** - `"Use this Google Gemini API snippet to generate product images, remember the integration for future use"` - agent learns and stores the solution in memory

- **Automated Server Monitoring** - `"Check server status every 30 minutes: CPU usage, disk space, memory. Alert if metrics exceed thresholds"` (scheduled task with project-scoped credentials)

- **Multi-Client Project Isolation** - Separate projects for each client with isolated memory, custom instructions, and dedicated secrets - prevents context bleed across sensitive work

## 🐳 Fully Dockerized, with Speech-to-Text and TTS

![Settings](docs/res/settings-page-ui1.png)

- Customizable settings allow users to tailor the agent's behavior and responses to their needs.
- The Web UI output is very clean, fluid, colorful, readable, and interactive; nothing is hidden.
- You can load or save chats directly within the Web UI.
- The same output you see in the terminal is automatically saved to an HTML file in **logs/** folder for every session.

![Time example](/docs/res/time_example.jpg)

- Agent output is streamed in real-time, allowing users to read along and intervene at any time.
- No coding is required; only prompting and communication skills are necessary.
- With a solid system prompt, the framework is reliable even with small models, including precise tool usage.

## 👀 Keep in Mind

1. **Agent Zero Can Be Dangerous!**

- With proper instruction, Agent Zero is capable of many things, even potentially dangerous actions concerning your computer, data, or accounts. Always run Agent Zero in an isolated environment (like Docker) and be careful what you wish for.

2. **Agent Zero Is Prompt-based.**

- The whole framework is guided by the **prompts/** folder. Agent guidelines, tool instructions, messages, utility AI functions, it's all there.


## 📚 Read the Documentation

| Page | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [Installation](./docs/setup/installation.md) | Installation, setup and configuration |
| [Usage](./docs/guides/usage.md) | Basic and advanced usage |

| [Development Setup](./docs/setup/dev-setup.md) | Development and customization |
| [WebSocket Infrastructure](./docs/developer/websockets.md) | Real-time WebSocket handlers, client APIs, filtering semantics, envelopes |
| [Extensions](./docs/developer/extensions.md) | Extending Agent Zero |
| [Connectivity](./docs/developer/connectivity.md) | External API endpoints, MCP server connections, A2A protocol |
| [Architecture](./docs/developer/architecture.md) | System design and components |
| [Contributing](./docs/guides/contribution.md) | How to contribute |
| [Troubleshooting](./docs/guides/troubleshooting.md) | Common issues and their solutions |

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
 Create a Daily Briefing report by fetching and analyzing data from Jira (via script), Google Chat (via script), and Gmail (via script).
# Solution
 1. Fetch Jira data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/jira-filter-listing/scripts/jira_filter_listing.py --filter-id 13811 --limit 100 > /a0/usr/workdir/jira_data.json'
2. Fetch Google Chat data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/gchat-thread-reader/scripts/gchat_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 300 --max-scroll 5 > /a0/usr/workdir/gchat_data.json'
3. Fetch Gmail data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 100 > /a0/usr/workdir/gmail_data.json'

# Problem
 Unable to view large JSON log files because standard 'cat' or 'document_query' tools resulted in terminal hangs, indexing errors, or truncated outputs.
# Solution
 Use a Python script to safely read and print file contents in a controlled manner:
1. Open the target file using Python's 'open' function.
2. Print the file content using 'f.read()'.
3. If the file is large, slice the output (e.g., 'f.read()[:1500]') to prevent terminal buffer overflows and ensure complete data retrieval.

kvps.memories:
User's primary workplace tools are Jira, Google Chat, and Gmail. Automated data collection scripts utilized for these tools: Jira filter listing (e.g., ticket fetching), GChat thread reader, and Gmail thread reader (located at /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py).

- **API Integration Without Code** - `"Use this Google Gemini API snippet to generate product images, remember the integration for future use"` - agent learns and stores the solution in memory

- **Automated Server Monitoring** - `"Check server status every 30 minutes: CPU usage, disk space, memory. Alert if metrics exceed thresholds"` (scheduled task with project-scoped credentials)

- **Multi-Client Project Isolation** - Separate projects for each client with isolated memory, custom instructions, and dedicated secrets - prevents context bleed across sensitive work

## 🐳 Fully Dockerized, with Speech-to-Text and TTS

![Settings](docs/res/settings-page-ui1.png)

- Customizable settings allow users to tailor the agent's behavior and responses to their needs.
- The Web UI output is very clean, fluid, colorful, readable, and interactive; nothing is hidden.
- You can load or save chats directly within the Web UI.
- The same output you see in the terminal is automatically saved to an HTML file in **logs/** folder for every session.

![Time example](/docs/res/time_example.jpg)

- Agent output is streamed in real-time, allowing users to read along and intervene at any time.
- No coding is required; only prompting and communication skills are necessary.
- With a solid system prompt, the framework is reliable even with small models, including precise tool usage.

## 👀 Keep in Mind

1. **Agent Zero Can Be Dangerous!**

- With proper instruction, Agent Zero is capable of many things, even potentially dangerous actions concerning your computer, data, or accounts. Always run Agent Zero in an isolated environment (like Docker) and be careful what you wish for.

2. **Agent Zero Is Prompt-based.**

- The whole framework is guided by the **prompts/** folder. Agent guidelines, tool instructions, messages, utility AI functions, it's all there.

kvps.solutions:
# Problem
 Create a Daily Briefing report by fetching and analyzing data from Jira (via script), Google Chat (via script), and Gmail (via script).
# Solution
 1. Fetch Jira data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/jira-filter-listing/scripts/jira_filter_listing.py --filter-id 13811 --limit 100 > /a0/usr/workdir/jira_data.json'
2. Fetch Google Chat data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/gchat-thread-reader/scripts/gchat_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 300 --max-scroll 5 > /a0/usr/workdir/gchat_data.json'
3. Fetch Gmail data: Run 'python /a0/usr/skills/gmail-thread-reader/scripts/gmail_thread_reader.py --days 5 --max-threads 100 > /a0/usr/workdir/gmail_data.json'
4. Analyze the content of the generated JSON files (using 'cat' to view contents if direct document querying fails).
5. Synthesize the collected data into the required format (Action Items, Follow-Ups, Summary) and save the final markdown file to 'workdir/❗ Daily Work Report [DATE].md' using 'cat << 'EOF' > [PATH]'.

# Problem
 Unable to view large JSON log files because standard 'cat' or 'document_query' tools resulted in terminal hangs, indexing errors, or truncated outputs.
# Solution
 Use a Python script to safely read and print file contents in a controlled manner:
1. Open the target file using Python's 'open' function.
2. Print the file content using 'f.read()'.
3. If the file is large, slice the output (e.g., 'f.read()[:1500]') to prevent terminal buffer overflows and ensure complete data retrieval.
